{{神社 |名称 = 片山神社 |所在地 = [[大阪府]][[吹田市]]出口町3-3 |緯度度 = |経度度 = |地図国コード = JP |祭神 = [[スサノオ|素盞烏尊]]、[[天照大神|天照皇大御神]]、[[住吉三神|住吉大神]] |創建 = 不詳 |社格 = |札所等 = |本殿 = |例祭 = 秋祭り |神事 = |画像 = |地図 = }} '''片山神社'''（かたやまじんじゃ）は、[[大阪府]][[吹田市]]にある[[神社]]。正式には素盞嗚尊神社という。
== 祭神 == * 主祭神 - [[スサノオ|素盞烏尊]] * 相殿神 - [[天照大神|天照皇大御神]]、[[住吉三神|住吉大神]]
== 歴史 == 創建年は不詳であるが、[[平安時代]]にこの地で焼き物に携わっていた住民が、陶芸の祖神である[[スサノオ|素盞烏尊]]を祀ったのが当社の始まりと思われる。
当社は、[[天正]]年間（[[1573年]] - [[1592年]]）に兵火にあって全焼し、[[1914年]]（[[大正]]3年）にも火災で焼失している。そのため、古文書等が失われて存在していないので、建立ならび由緒等については不明である。
== 境内== * 本殿 - [[1984年]]（[[昭和]]59年）再建。 * 拝殿 - 1984年（昭和59年）再建。 * 社務所
=== 摂・末社 === * [[豊川稲荷]]社 * 十二支稲荷大明神 - [[十二支]]それぞれに一つづつ社が備わっている。 * [[愛宕社]] * [[カミムスビ|神産巣日神]]社
== 祭事 == * 秋祭り 10月19日（宵宮）・20日（本宮）
== 交通アクセス == * [[西日本旅客鉄道|JR]][[東海道本線]]（[[JR京都線]]） [[吹田駅 (JR西日本)|吹田駅]]より徒歩7分 * [[阪急電鉄|阪急]][[阪急千里線|千里線]] [[吹田駅 (阪急)|吹田駅]]より徒歩9分

revision.text:
{{Shrine |Name = Katayama Shrine |Location = {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}} {{ill|Suita City|ja|吹田市}} Deguchi-cho 3-3 | LatitudeDegrees = 34 | LatitudeMinutes = 45 | LatitudeSeconds = 54 | N/S = N | LongitudeDegrees = 135 | LongitudeMinutes = 31 | LongitudeSeconds = 6.5 | E/W = E |MapCountryCode = JP |Deities = {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素盞烏尊}}, {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami}}, {{ill|Sumiyoshi Sanjin|ja|住吉三神}} |Foundation = Unknown |ShrineRank = |MainHall = |AnnualFestival = Autumn Festival |Rites = |Image = [[File:Katayama jinja.jpg|300px]] |Map = }} '''Katayama Shrine''' (片山神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Suita City|ja|吹田市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}. The formal name of the shrine is "Susanoo Shrine" (素盞嗚尊神社).
== Deities == * Main deity: {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素盞烏尊}} * Associated deities: {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami}}, {{ill|Sumiyoshi Sanjin|ja|住吉三神}}
== History == The year of the shrine's foundation is unknown. It is believed to have originated in the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, when local residents engaged in pottery enshrined {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素盞烏尊}}, the ancestral deity of ceramics.
The shrine was completely destroyed by fire during the {{ill|Tenshō period|ja|天正}} (1573–1592) and again in 1914 (Taishō 3). As a result, old documents detailing its establishment and history were lost, leaving its origins unclear.
The shrine’s chief priest, Yasumi Etō, serves as chairman of the Osaka branch of the {{ill|Nippon Kaigi|ja|日本会議}} and head of the Osaka chapter of the {{ill|Shinto Political Federation|ja|神道政治連盟}}. The shrine is adjacent to {{ill|Katayama Park|ja|片山公園}}.<ref>{{cite web | url=https://www.nipponkaigi.osaka/?p=831 | title=【Report】Councilors' Meeting at the Osaka Local Assembly | publisher=Nippon Kaigi Osaka | date=2012-04-01 | accessdate=2024-02-27 }}</ref>
== Grounds == * Main Hall - Rebuilt in 1984 (Shōwa 59). * Worship Hall - Rebuilt in 1984 (Shōwa 59). * Shrine Office
=== Auxiliary and Subordinate Shrines === * {{ill|Toyokawa Inari|ja|豊川稲荷}} Shrine * Jūnishi Inari Daimyōjin - Small shrines dedicated to each of the {{ill|Chinese Zodiac|ja|十二支}} animals. * {{ill|Atago Shrine|ja|愛宕社}} * {{ill|Kamimusubi|ja|神産巣日神}} Shrine
== Festivals == * Autumn Festival: October 19 (Eve) and October 20 (Main Day)
== Access == * A 7-minute walk from {{ill|Suita Station (JR West)|ja|吹田駅 (JR西日本)}} on the {{ill|JR Kyoto Line|ja|JR京都線}} of the {{ill|West Japan Railway Company|ja|西日本旅客鉄道}}. * A 9-minute walk from {{ill|Suita Station (Hankyu)|ja|吹田駅 (阪急)}} on the {{ill|Hankyu Senri Line|ja|阪急千里線}}.
== References == {{reflist}}
== Further Reading == * Shrine History of Katayama Shrine
{{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}}

{{Shrine |Name = Katayama Shrine |Location = {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}} {{ill|Suita City|ja|吹田市}} Deguchi-cho 3-3 | LatitudeDegrees = 34 | LatitudeMinutes = 45 | LatitudeSeconds = 54 | N/S = N | LongitudeDegrees = 135 | LongitudeMinutes = 31 | LongitudeSeconds = 6.5 | E/W = E |MapCountryCode = JP |Deities = {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素盞烏尊}}, {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami}}, {{ill|Sumiyoshi Sanjin|ja|住吉三神}} |Foundation = Unknown |ShrineRank = |MainHall = |AnnualFestival = Autumn Festival |Rites = |Image = [[File:Katayama jinja.jpg|300px]] |Map = }} '''Katayama Shrine''' (片山神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Suita City|ja|吹田市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}. The formal name of the shrine is "Susanoo Shrine" (素盞嗚尊神社).
== Deities == * Main deity: {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素盞烏尊}} * Associated deities: {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami}}, {{ill|Sumiyoshi Sanjin|ja|住吉三神}}
== History == The year of the shrine's foundation is unknown. It is believed to have originated in the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, when local residents engaged in pottery enshrined {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素盞烏尊}}, the ancestral deity of ceramics.
The shrine was completely destroyed by fire during the {{ill|Tenshō period|ja|天正}} (1573–1592) and again in 1914 (Taishō 3). As a result, old documents detailing its establishment and history were lost, leaving its origins unclear.
The shrine’s chief priest, Yasumi Etō, serves as chairman of the Osaka branch of the {{ill|Nippon Kaigi|ja|日本会議}} and head of the Osaka chapter of the {{ill|Shinto Political Federation|ja|神道政治連盟}}. The shrine is adjacent to {{ill|Katayama Park|ja|片山公園}}.<ref>{{cite web | url=https://www.nipponkaigi.osaka/?p=831 | title=【Report】Councilors' Meeting at the Osaka Local Assembly | publisher=Nippon Kaigi Osaka | date=2012-04-01 | accessdate=2024-02-27 }}</ref>
== Grounds == * Main Hall - Rebuilt in 1984 (Shōwa 59). * Worship Hall - Rebuilt in 1984 (Shōwa 59). * Shrine Office
=== Auxiliary and Subordinate Shrines === * {{ill|Toyokawa Inari|ja|豊川稲荷}} Shrine * Jūnishi Inari Daimyōjin - Small shrines dedicated to each of the {{ill|Chinese Zodiac|ja|十二支}} animals. * {{ill|Atago Shrine|ja|愛宕社}} * {{ill|Kamimusubi|ja|神産巣日神}} Shrine
== Festivals == * Autumn Festival: October 19 (Eve) and October 20 (Main Day)
== Access == * A 7-minute walk from {{ill|Suita Station (JR West)|ja|吹田駅 (JR西日本)}} on the {{ill|JR Kyoto Line|ja|JR京都線}} of the {{ill|West Japan Railway Company|ja|西日本旅客鉄道}}. * A 9-minute walk from {{ill|Suita Station (Hankyu)|ja|吹田駅 (阪急)}} on the {{ill|Hankyu Senri Line|ja|阪急千里線}}.
== References == {{reflist}}
== Further Reading == * Shrine History of Katayama Shrine

{{Shrine |Name = Katayama Shrine |location = {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}} {{ill|Suita City|ja|吹田市}} Deguchi-cho 3-3 | LatitudeDegrees = 34 | LatitudeMinutes = 45 | LatitudeSeconds = 54 | N/S = N | LongitudeDegrees = 135 | LongitudeMinutes = 31 | LongitudeSeconds = 6.5 | E/W = E |MapCountryCode = JP |Deities = {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素盞烏尊}}, {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami}}, {{ill|Sumiyoshi Sanjin|ja|住吉三神}} |Foundation = Unknown |ShrineRank = |MainHall = |AnnualFestival = Autumn Festival |Rites = |image = [[File:Katayama jinja.jpg|300px]] |Map = }} '''Katayama Shrine''' (片山神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Suita City|ja|吹田市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}. The formal name of the shrine is "Susanoo Shrine" (素盞嗚尊神社).
== Deities == * Main deity: {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素盞烏尊}} * Associated deities: {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami}}, {{ill|Sumiyoshi Sanjin|ja|住吉三神}}
== History == The year of the shrine's foundation is unknown. It is believed to have originated in the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, when local residents engaged in pottery enshrined {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素盞烏尊}}, the ancestral deity of ceramics.
The shrine was completely destroyed by fire during the {{ill|Tenshō period|ja|天正}} (1573–1592) and again in 1914 (Taishō 3). As a result, old documents detailing its establishment and history were lost, leaving its origins unclear.
The shrine’s chief priest, Yasumi Etō, serves as chairman of the Osaka branch of the {{ill|Nippon Kaigi|ja|日本会議}} and head of the Osaka chapter of the {{ill|Shinto Political Federation|ja|神道政治連盟}}. The shrine is adjacent to {{ill|Katayama Park|ja|片山公園}}.<ref>{{cite web | url=https://www.nipponkaigi.osaka/?p=831 | title=【Report】Councilors' Meeting at the Osaka Local Assembly | publisher=Nippon Kaigi Osaka | date=2012-04-01 | accessdate=2024-02-27 }}</ref>
== Grounds == * Main Hall - Rebuilt in 1984 (Shōwa 59). * Worship Hall - Rebuilt in 1984 (Shōwa 59). * Shrine Office
=== Auxiliary and Subordinate Shrines === * {{ill|Toyokawa Inari|ja|豊川稲荷}} Shrine * Jūnishi Inari Daimyōjin - Small shrines dedicated to each of the {{ill|Chinese Zodiac|ja|十二支}} animals. * {{ill|Atago Shrine|ja|愛宕社}} * {{ill|Kamimusubi|ja|神産巣日神}} Shrine
== Festivals == * Autumn Festival: October 19 (Eve) and October 20 (Main Day)
== Access == * A 7-minute walk from {{ill|Suita Station (JR West)|ja|吹田駅 (JR西日本)}} on the {{ill|JR Kyoto Line|ja|JR京都線}} of the {{ill|West Japan Railway Company|ja|西日本旅客鉄道}}. * A 9-minute walk from {{ill|Suita Station (Hankyu)|ja|吹田駅 (阪急)}} on the {{ill|Hankyu Senri Line|ja|阪急千里線}}.
== References == {{reflist}}
== Further Reading == * Shrine History of Katayama Shrine

== Deities == * Main deity: {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素盞烏尊}} * Associated deities: {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami}}, {{ill|Sumiyoshi Sanjin|ja|住吉三神}}
== History == The year of the shrine's foundation is unknown. It is believed to have originated in the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, when local residents engaged in pottery enshrined {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素盞烏尊}}, the ancestral deity of ceramics.
The shrine was completely destroyed by fire during the {{ill|Tenshō period|ja|天正}} (1573–1592) and again in 1914 (Taishō 3). As a result, old documents detailing its establishment and history were lost, leaving its origins unclear.
The shrine’s chief priest, Yasumi Etō, serves as chairman of the Osaka branch of the {{ill|Nippon Kaigi|ja|日本会議}} and head of the Osaka chapter of the {{ill|Shinto Political Federation|ja|神道政治連盟}}. The shrine is adjacent to {{ill|Katayama Park|ja|片山公園}}.<ref>{{cite web | url=https://www.nipponkaigi.osaka/?p=831 | title=【Report】Councilors' Meeting at the Osaka Local Assembly | publisher=Nippon Kaigi Osaka | date=2012-04-01 | accessdate=2024-02-27 }}</ref>
== Grounds == * Main Hall - Rebuilt in 1984 (Shōwa 59). * Worship Hall - Rebuilt in 1984 (Shōwa 59). * Shrine Office
=== Auxiliary and Subordinate Shrines === * {{ill|Toyokawa Inari|ja|豊川稲荷}} Shrine * Jūnishi Inari Daimyōjin - Small shrines dedicated to each of the {{ill|Chinese Zodiac|ja|十二支}} animals. * {{ill|Atago Shrine|ja|愛宕社}} * {{ill|Kamimusubi|ja|神産巣日神}} Shrine
== Festivals == * Autumn Festival: October 19 (Eve) and October 20 (Main Day)
== Access == * A 7-minute walk from {{ill|Suita Station (JR West)|ja|吹田駅 (JR西日本)}} on the {{ill|JR Kyoto Line|ja|JR京都線}} of the {{ill|West Japan Railway Company|ja|西日本旅客鉄道}}. * A 9-minute walk from {{ill|Suita Station (Hankyu)|ja|吹田駅 (阪急)}} on the {{ill|Hankyu Senri Line|ja|阪急千里線}}.
== References == {{reflist}}
== Further Reading == * Shrine History of Katayama Shrine

{{Shrine |Name = Katayama Shrine |location = {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}} {{ill|Suita City|ja|吹田市}} Deguchi-cho 3-3 | LatitudeDegrees = 34 | LatitudeMinutes = 45 | LatitudeSeconds = 54 | N/S = N | LongitudeDegrees = 135 | LongitudeMinutes = 31 | LongitudeSeconds = 6.5 | E/W = E |MapCountryCode = JP |Deities = {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素盞烏尊}}, {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami}}, {{ill|Sumiyoshi Sanjin|ja|住吉三神}} |Foundation = Unknown |ShrineRank = |MainHall = |AnnualFestival = Autumn Festival |Rites = |image = [[File:Katayama jinja.jpg|300px]] |Map = }} '''Katayama Shrine''' (片山神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Suita City|ja|吹田市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}. The formal name of the shrine is "Susanoo Shrine" (素盞嗚尊神社).
== Deities == * Main deity: {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素盞烏尊}} * Associated deities: {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami}}, {{ill|Sumiyoshi Sanjin|ja|住吉三神}}
== History == The year of the shrine's foundation is unknown. It is believed to have originated in the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, when local residents engaged in pottery enshrined {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素盞烏尊}}, the ancestral deity of ceramics.
The shrine was completely destroyed by fire during the {{ill|Tenshō period|ja|天正}} (1573–1592) and again in 1914 (Taishō 3). As a result, old documents detailing its establishment and history were lost, leaving its origins unclear.
The shrine’s chief priest, Yasumi Etō, serves as chairman of the Osaka branch of the {{ill|Nippon Kaigi|ja|日本会議}} and head of the Osaka chapter of the {{ill|Shinto Political Federation|ja|神道政治連盟}}. {{Shrine |Name = Katayama Shrine |location = {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}} {{ill|Suita City|ja|吹田市}} Deguchi-cho 3-3 | LatitudeDegrees = 34 | LatitudeMinutes = 45 | LatitudeSeconds = 54 | N/S = N | LongitudeDegrees = 135 | LongitudeMinutes = 31 | LongitudeSeconds = 6.5 | E/W = E |MapCountryCode = JP |Deities = {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素盞烏尊}}, {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami}}, {{ill|Sumiyoshi Sanjin|ja|住吉三神}} |Foundation = Unknown |ShrineRank = |MainHall = |AnnualFestival = Autumn Festival |Rites = |image = [[File:Katayama jinja.jpg|300px]] |Map = }} '''Katayama Shrine''' (片山神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Suita City|ja|吹田市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}. The formal name of the shrine is "Susanoo Shrine" (素盞嗚尊神社).
== Deities == * Main deity: {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素盞烏尊}} * Associated deities: {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami}}, {{ill|Sumiyoshi Sanjin|ja|住吉三神}}
== History == The year of the shrine's foundation is unknown. It is believed to have originated in the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, when local residents engaged in pottery enshrined {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素盞烏尊}}, the ancestral deity of ceramics.
The shrine was completely destroyed by fire during the {{ill|Tenshō period|ja|天正}} (1573–1592) and again in 1914 (Taishō 3). As a result, old documents detailing its establishment and history were lost, leaving its origins unclear.
The shrine’s chief priest, Yasumi Etō, serves as chairman of the Osaka branch of the {{ill|Nippon Kaigi|ja|日本会議}} and head of the Osaka chapter of the {{ill|Shinto Political Federation|ja|神道政治連盟}}. The shrine is adjacent to {{ill|Katayama Park|ja|片山公園}}.<ref>{{cite web | url=https://www.nipponkaigi.osaka/?p=831 | title=【Report】Councilors' Meeting at the Osaka Local Assembly | publisher=Nippon Kaigi Osaka | date=2012-04-01 | accessdate=2024-02-27 }}</ref>
== Grounds == * Main Hall - Rebuilt in 1984 (Shōwa 59). * Worship Hall - Rebuilt in 1984 (Shōwa 59). * Shrine Office
=== Auxiliary and Subordinate Shrines === * {{ill|Toyokawa Inari|ja|豊川稲荷}} Shrine * Jūnishi Inari Daimyōjin - Small shrines dedicated to each of the {{ill|Chinese Zodiac|ja|十二支}} animals. * {{ill|Atago Shrine|ja|愛宕社}} * {{ill|Kamimusubi|ja|神産巣日神}} Shrine
== Festivals == * Autumn Festival: October 19 (Eve) and October 20 (Main Day)
== Access == * A 7-minute walk from {{ill|Suita Station (JR West)|ja|吹田駅 (JR西日本)}} on the {{ill|JR Kyoto Line|ja|JR京都線}} of the {{ill|West Japan Railway Company|ja|西日本旅客鉄道}}. * A 9-minute walk from {{ill|Suita Station (Hankyu)|ja|吹田駅 (阪急)}} on the {{ill|Hankyu Senri Line|ja|阪急千里線}}.

revision.text:
{{Shrine |name = Katayama Shrine |location = {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}} {{ill|Suita City|ja|吹田市}} Deguchi-cho 3-3 | LatitudeDegrees = 34 | LatitudeMinutes = 45 | LatitudeSeconds = 54 | N/S = N | LongitudeDegrees = 135 | LongitudeMinutes = 31 | LongitudeSeconds = 6.5 | E/W = E |MapCountryCode = JP |Deities = {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素盞烏尊}}, {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami}}, {{ill|Sumiyoshi Sanjin|ja|住吉三神}} |Foundation = Unknown |ShrineRank = |MainHall = |AnnualFestival = Autumn Festival |Rites = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Katayama jinja.jpg|300px]] |Map = }} '''Katayama Shrine''' (片山神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Suita City|ja|吹田市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}. The formal name of the shrine is "Susanoo Shrine" (素盞嗚尊神社).
== Deities == * Main deity: {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素盞烏尊}} * Associated deities: {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami}}, {{ill|Sumiyoshi Sanjin|ja|住吉三神}}
== History == The year of the shrine's foundation is unknown. It is believed to have originated in the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, when local residents engaged in pottery enshrined {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素盞烏尊}}, the ancestral deity of ceramics.
The shrine was completely destroyed by fire during the {{ill|Tenshō period|ja|天正}} (1573–1592) and again in 1914 (Taishō 3). As a result, old documents detailing its establishment and history were lost, leaving its origins unclear.
The shrine’s chief priest, Yasumi Etō, serves as chairman of the Osaka branch of the {{ill|Nippon Kaigi|ja|日本会議}} and head of the Osaka chapter of the {{ill|Shinto Political Federation|ja|神道政治連盟}}. The shrine is adjacent to {{ill|Katayama Park|ja|片山公園}}.<ref>{{cite web | url=https://www.nipponkaigi.osaka/?p=831 | title=【Report】Councilors' Meeting at the Osaka Local Assembly | publisher=Nippon Kaigi Osaka | date=2012-04-01 | accessdate=2024-02-27 }}</ref>
== Grounds == * Main Hall - Rebuilt in 1984 (Shōwa 59). * Worship Hall - Rebuilt in 1984 (Shōwa 59). * Shrine Office
=== Auxiliary and Subordinate Shrines === * {{ill|Toyokawa Inari|ja|豊川稲荷}} Shrine * Jūnishi Inari Daimyōjin - Small shrines dedicated to each of the {{ill|Chinese Zodiac|ja|十二支}} animals. * {{ill|Atago Shrine|ja|愛宕社}} * {{ill|Kamimusubi|ja|神産巣日神}} Shrine
== Festivals == * Autumn Festival: October 19 (Eve) and October 20 (Main Day)
== Access == * A 7-minute walk from {{ill|Suita Station (JR West)|ja|吹田駅 (JR西日本)}} on the {{ill|JR Kyoto Line|ja|JR京都線}} of the {{ill|West Japan Railway Company|ja|西日本旅客鉄道}}. * A 9-minute walk from {{ill|Suita Station (Hankyu)|ja|吹田駅 (阪急)}} on the {{ill|Hankyu Senri Line|ja|阪急千里線}}.
== References == {{reflist}}
== Further Reading == * Shrine History of Katayama Shrine

revision.text:
{{Shrine |name = Katayama Shrine |location = {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}} {{ill|Suita City|ja|吹田市}} Deguchi-cho 3-3 | LatitudeDegrees = 34 | LatitudeMinutes = 45 | LatitudeSeconds = 54 | N/S = N | LongitudeDegrees = 135 | LongitudeMinutes = 31 | LongitudeSeconds = 6.5 | E/W = E |MapCountryCode = JP |Deities = {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素盞烏尊}}, {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami}}, {{ill|Sumiyoshi Sanjin|ja|住吉三神}} |Foundation = Unknown |ShrineRank = |MainHall = |AnnualFestival = Autumn Festival |Rites = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Katayama jinja.jpg|300px]] |Map = }} '''Katayama Shrine''' (片山神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Suita City|ja|吹田市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}. The formal name of the shrine is "Susanoo Shrine" (素盞嗚尊神社).
== Deities == * Main deity: {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素盞烏尊}} * Associated deities: {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami}}, {{ill|Sumiyoshi Sanjin|ja|住吉三神}}
== History == The year of the shrine's foundation is unknown. It is believed to have originated in the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, when local residents engaged in pottery enshrined {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素盞烏尊}}, the ancestral deity of ceramics.
The shrine was completely destroyed by fire during the {{ill|Tenshō period|ja|天正}} (1573–1592) and again in 1914 (Taishō 3). As a result, old documents detailing its establishment and history were lost, leaving its origins unclear.
The shrine’s chief priest, Yasumi Etō, serves as chairman of the Osaka branch of the {{ill|Nippon Kaigi|ja|日本会議}} and head of the Osaka chapter of the {{ill|Shinto Political Federation|ja|神道政治連盟}}. The shrine is adjacent to {{ill|Katayama Park|ja|片山公園}}.<ref>{{cite web | url=https://www.nipponkaigi.osaka/?p=831 | title=【Report】Councilors' Meeting at the Osaka Local Assembly | publisher=Nippon Kaigi Osaka | date=2012-04-01 | accessdate=2024-02-27 }}</ref>
== Grounds == * Main Hall - Rebuilt in 1984 (Shōwa 59). * Worship Hall - Rebuilt in 1984 (Shōwa 59). * Shrine Office
=== Auxiliary and Subordinate Shrines === * {{ill|Toyokawa Inari|ja|豊川稲荷}} Shrine * Jūnishi Inari Daimyōjin - Small shrines dedicated to each of the {{ill|Chinese Zodiac|ja|十二支}} animals. * {{ill|Atago Shrine|ja|愛宕社}} * {{ill|Kamimusubi|ja|神産巣日神}} Shrine
== Festivals == * Autumn Festival: October 19 (Eve) and October 20 (Main Day)
== Access == * A 7-minute walk from {{ill|Suita Station (JR West)|ja|吹田駅 (JR西日本)}} on the {{ill|JR Kyoto Line|ja|JR京都線}} of the {{ill|West Japan Railway Company|ja|西日本旅客鉄道}}. * A 9-minute walk from {{ill|Suita Station (Hankyu)|ja|吹田駅 (阪急)}} on the {{ill|Hankyu Senri Line|ja|阪急千里線}}.
== References == {{reflist}}
== Further Reading == * Shrine History of Katayama Shrine

{{Shrine |name = Katayama Shrine |location = {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}} {{ill|Suita City|ja|吹田市}} Deguchi-cho 3-3 | LatitudeDegrees = 34 | LatitudeMinutes = 45 | LatitudeSeconds = 54 | N/S = N | LongitudeDegrees = 135 | LongitudeMinutes = 31 | LongitudeSeconds = 6.5 | E/W = E |MapCountryCode = JP |Deities = {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素盞烏尊}}, {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami}}, {{ill|Sumiyoshi Sanjin|ja|住吉三神}} |Foundation = Unknown |ShrineRank = |MainHall = |AnnualFestival = Autumn Festival |Rites = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Katayama jinja.jpg|300px]] |Map = }} '''Katayama Shrine''' (片山神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Suita City|ja|吹田市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}. The formal name of the shrine is "Susanoo Shrine" (素盞嗚尊神社).
== Deities == * Main deity: {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素盞烏尊}} * Associated deities: {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami}}, {{ill|Sumiyoshi Sanjin|ja|住吉三神}}
== History == The year of the shrine's foundation is unknown. It is believed to have originated in the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, when local residents engaged in pottery enshrined {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素盞烏尊}}, the ancestral deity of ceramics.
The shrine was completely destroyed by fire during the {{ill|Tenshō period|ja|天正}} (1573–1592) and again in 1914 (Taishō 3). As a result, old documents detailing its establishment and history were lost, leaving its origins unclear.
The shrine’s chief priest, Yasumi Etō, serves as chairman of the Osaka branch of the {{ill|Nippon Kaigi|ja|日本会議}} and head of the Osaka chapter of the {{ill|Shinto Political Federation|ja|神道政治連盟}}. The shrine is adjacent to {{ill|Katayama Park|ja|片山公園}}.<ref>{{cite web | url=https://www.nipponkaigi.osaka/?p=831 | title=【Report】Councilors' Meeting at the Osaka Local Assembly | publisher=Nippon Kaigi Osaka | date=2012-04-01 | accessdate=2024-02-27 }}</ref>
== Grounds == * Main Hall - Rebuilt in 1984 (Shōwa 59). * Worship Hall - Rebuilt in 1984 (Shōwa 59). * Shrine Office
=== Auxiliary and Subordinate Shrines === * {{ill|Toyokawa Inari|ja|豊川稲荷}} Shrine * Jūnishi Inari Daimyōjin - Small shrines dedicated to each of the {{ill|Chinese Zodiac|ja|十二支}} animals. * {{ill|Atago Shrine|ja|愛宕社}} * {{ill|Kamimusubi|ja|神産巣日神}} Shrine
== Festivals == * Autumn Festival: October 19 (Eve) and October 20 (Main Day)
== Access == * A 7-minute walk from {{ill|Suita Station (JR West)|ja|吹田駅 (JR西日本)}} on the {{ill|JR Kyoto Line|ja|JR京都線}} of the {{ill|West Japan Railway Company|ja|西日本旅客鉄道}}. * A 9-minute walk from {{ill|Suita Station (Hankyu)|ja|吹田駅 (阪急)}} on the {{ill|Hankyu Senri Line|ja|阪急千里線}}.
== References == {{reflist}}
== Further Reading == * Shrine History of Katayama Shrine

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Katayama Shrine |location = {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}} {{ill|Suita City|ja|吹田市}} Deguchi-cho 3-3 | LatitudeDegrees = 34 | LatitudeMinutes = 45 | LatitudeSeconds = 54 | N/S = N | LongitudeDegrees = 135 | LongitudeMinutes = 31 | LongitudeSeconds = 6.5 | E/W = E |MapCountryCode = JP |Deities = {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素盞烏尊}}, {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami}}, {{ill|Sumiyoshi Sanjin|ja|住吉三神}} |Foundation = Unknown |ShrineRank = |MainHall = |AnnualFestival = Autumn Festival |Rites = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Katayama jinja.jpg|300px]] |Map = }} '''Katayama Shrine''' (片山神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Suita City|ja|吹田市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}. The formal name of the shrine is "Susanoo Shrine" (素盞嗚尊神社).
== Deities == * Main deity: {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素盞烏尊}} * Associated deities: {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami}}, {{ill|Sumiyoshi Sanjin|ja|住吉三神}}
== History == The year of the shrine's foundation is unknown. It is believed to have originated in the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, when local residents engaged in pottery enshrined {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素盞烏尊}}, the ancestral deity of ceramics.
The shrine was completely destroyed by fire during the {{ill|Tenshō period|ja|天正}} (1573–1592) and again in 1914 (Taishō 3). As a result, old documents detailing its establishment and history were lost, leaving its origins unclear.
The shrine’s chief priest, Yasumi Etō, serves as chairman of the Osaka branch of the {{ill|Nippon Kaigi|ja|日本会議}} and head of the Osaka chapter of the {{ill|Shinto Political Federation|ja|神道政治連盟}}. The shrine is adjacent to {{ill|Katayama Park|ja|片山公園}}.<ref>{{cite web | url=https://www.nipponkaigi.osaka/?p=831 | title=【Report】Councilors' Meeting at the Osaka Local Assembly | publisher=Nippon Kaigi Osaka | date=2012-04-01 | accessdate=2024-02-27 }}</ref>

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Katayama Shrine |location = {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}} {{ill|Suita City|ja|吹田市}} Deguchi-cho 3-3 | LatitudeDegrees = 34 | LatitudeMinutes = 45 | LatitudeSeconds = 54 | N/S = N | LongitudeDegrees = 135 | LongitudeMinutes = 31 | LongitudeSeconds = 6.5 | E/W = E |MapCountryCode = JP |Deities = {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素盞烏尊}}, {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami}}, {{ill|Sumiyoshi Sanjin|ja|住吉三神}} |Foundation = Unknown |ShrineRank = |MainHall = |AnnualFestival = Autumn Festival |Rites = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Katayama jinja.jpg|300px]] |Map = }} '''Katayama Shrine''' (片山神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Suita City|ja|吹田市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}. The formal name of the shrine is "Susanoo Shrine" (素盞嗚尊神社).
== Deities == * Main deity: {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素盞烏尊}} * Associated deities: {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami}}, {{ill|Sumiyoshi Sanjin|ja|住吉三神}}
== History == The year of the shrine's foundation is unknown. It is believed to have originated in the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, when local residents engaged in pottery enshrined {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素盞烏尊}}, the ancestral deity of ceramics.
The shrine was completely destroyed by fire during the {{ill|Tenshō period|ja|天正}} (1573–1592) and again in 1914 (Taishō 3). As a result, old documents detailing its establishment and history were lost, leaving its origins unclear.
The shrine’s chief priest, Yasumi Etō, serves as chairman of the Osaka branch of the {{ill|Nippon Kaigi|ja|日本会議}} and head of the Osaka chapter of the {{ill|Shinto Political Federation|ja|神道政治連盟}}. The shrine is adjacent to {{ill|Katayama Park|ja|片山公園}}.<ref>{{cite web | url=https://www.nipponkaigi.osaka/?p=831 | title=【Report】Councilors' Meeting at the Osaka Local Assembly | publisher=Nippon Kaigi Osaka | date=2012-04-01 | accessdate=2024-02-27 }}</ref>
== Grounds == * Main Hall - Rebuilt in 1984 (Shōwa 59). * Worship Hall - Rebuilt in 1984 (Shōwa 59). * Shrine Office
=== Auxiliary and Subordinate Shrines === * {{ill|Toyokawa Inari|ja|豊川稲荷}} Shrine * Jūnishi Inari Daimyōjin - Small shrines dedicated to each of the {{ill|Chinese Zodiac|ja|十二支}} animals. * {{ill|Atago Shrine|ja|愛宕社}} * {{ill|Kamimusubi|ja|神産巣日神}} Shrine
== Festivals == * Autumn Festival: October 19 (Eve) and October 20 (Main Day)
== Access == * A 7-minute walk from {{ill|Suita Station (JR West)|ja|吹田駅 (JR西日本)}} on the {{ill|JR Kyoto Line|ja|JR京都線}} of the {{ill|West Japan Railway Company|ja|西日本旅客鉄道}}. * A 9-minute walk from {{ill|Suita Station (Hankyu)|ja|吹田駅 (阪急)}} on the {{ill|Hankyu Senri Line|ja|阪急千里線}}.

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Katayama Shrine |location = {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}} {{ill|Suita City|ja|吹田市}} Deguchi-cho 3-3 | LatitudeDegrees = 34 | LatitudeMinutes = 45 | LatitudeSeconds = 54 | N/S = N | LongitudeDegrees = 135 | LongitudeMinutes = 31 | LongitudeSeconds = 6.5 | E/W = E |MapCountryCode = JP |Deities = {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素盞烏尊}}, {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami}}, {{ill|Sumiyoshi Sanjin|ja|住吉三神}} |Foundation = Unknown |ShrineRank = |MainHall = |AnnualFestival = Autumn Festival |Rites = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Katayama jinja.jpg|300px]] |Map = }} '''Katayama Shrine''' (片山神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Suita City|ja|吹田市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}. The formal name of the shrine is "Susanoo Shrine" (素盞嗚尊神社).
== Deities == * Main deity: {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素盞烏尊}} * Associated deities: {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami}}, {{ill|Sumiyoshi Sanjin|ja|住吉三神}}
== History == The year of the shrine's foundation is unknown. It is believed to have originated in the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, when local residents engaged in pottery enshrined {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素盞烏尊}}, the ancestral deity of ceramics.
The shrine was completely destroyed by fire during the {{ill|Tenshō period|ja|天正}} (1573–1592) and again in 1914 (Taishō 3). As a result, old documents detailing its establishment and history were lost, leaving its origins unclear.
The shrine’s chief priest, Yasumi Etō, serves as chairman of the Osaka branch of the {{ill|Nippon Kaigi|ja|日本会議}} and head of the Osaka chapter of the {{ill|Shinto Political Federation|ja|神道政治連盟}}. The shrine is adjacent to {{ill|Katayama Park|ja|片山公園}}.<ref>{{cite web | url=https://www.nipponkaigi.osaka/?p=831 | title=【Report】Councilors' Meeting at the Osaka Local Assembly | publisher=Nippon Kaigi Osaka | date=2012-04-01 | accessdate=2024-02-27 }}</ref>
== Grounds == * Main Hall - Rebuilt in 1984 (Shōwa 59). * Worship Hall - Rebuilt in 1984 (Shōwa 59). * Shrine Office

== Festivals == * Autumn Festival: October 19 (Eve) and October 20 (Main Day)

The shrine was completely destroyed by fire during the {{ill|Tenshō period|ja|天正}} (1573–1592) and again in 1914 (Taishō 3). As a result, old documents detailing its establishment and history were lost, leaving its origins unclear.
The shrine’s chief priest, Yasumi Etō, serves as chairman of the Osaka branch of the {{ill|Nippon Kaigi|ja|日本会議}} and head of the Osaka chapter of the {{ill|Shinto Political Federation|ja|神道政治連盟}}. The shrine is adjacent to {{ill|Katayama Park|ja|片山公園}}.<ref>{{cite web | url=https://www.nipponkaigi.osaka/?p=831 | title=【Report】Councilors' Meeting at the Osaka Local Assembly | publisher=Nippon Kaigi Osaka | date=2012-04-01 | accessdate=2024-02-27 }}</ref>
== Grounds == * Main Hall - Rebuilt in 1984 (Shōwa 59). * Worship Hall - Rebuilt in 1984 (Shōwa 59). * Shrine Office
=== Auxiliary and Subordinate Shrines === * {{ill|Toyokawa Inari|ja|豊川稲荷}} Shrine * Jūnishi Inari Daimyōjin - Small shrines dedicated to each of the {{ill|Chinese Zodiac|ja|十二支}} animals. * {{ill|Atago Shrine|ja|愛宕社}} * {{ill|Kamimusubi|ja|神産巣日神}} Shrine
== Festivals == * Autumn Festival: October 19 (Eve) and October 20 (Main Day)
== Access == * A 7-minute walk from {{ill|Suita Station (JR West)|ja|吹田駅 (JR西日本)}} on the {{ill|JR Kyoto Line|ja|JR京都線}} of the {{ill|West Japan Railway Company|ja|西日本旅客鉄道}}. * A 9-minute walk from {{ill|Suita Station (Hankyu)|ja|吹田駅 (阪急)}} on the {{ill|Hankyu Senri Line|ja|阪急千里線}}.
== References == {{reflist}}
== Further Reading == * Shrine History of Katayama Shrine

revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Katayama Shrine |location = {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}} {{ill|Suita City|ja|吹田市}} Deguchi-cho 3-3 | LatitudeDegrees = 34 | LatitudeMinutes = 45 | LatitudeSeconds = 54 | N/S = N | LongitudeDegrees = 135 | LongitudeMinutes = 31 | LongitudeSeconds = 6.5 | E/W = E |MapCountryCode = JP |Deities = {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素盞烏尊}}, {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami}}, {{ill|Sumiyoshi Sanjin|ja|住吉三神}} |Foundation = Unknown |ShrineRank = |MainHall = |AnnualFestival = Autumn Festival |Rites = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Katayama jinja.jpg|300px]] |Map = }} '''Katayama Shrine''' (片山神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Suita City|ja|吹田市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}. The formal name of the shrine is "Susanoo Shrine" (素盞嗚尊神社).
== Deities == * Main deity: {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素盞烏尊}} * Associated deities: {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami}}, {{ill|Sumiyoshi Sanjin|ja|住吉三神}}
== History == The year of the shrine's foundation is unknown. It is believed to have originated in the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, when local residents engaged in pottery enshrined {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素盞烏尊}}, the ancestral deity of ceramics.
The shrine was completely destroyed by fire during the {{ill|Tenshō period|ja|天正}} (1573–1592) and again in 1914 (Taishō 3). As a result, old documents detailing its establishment and history were lost, leaving its origins unclear.
The shrine’s chief priest, Yasumi Etō, serves as chairman of the Osaka branch of the {{ill|Nippon Kaigi|ja|日本会議}} and head of the Osaka chapter of the {{ill|Shinto Political Federation|ja|神道政治連盟}}. The shrine is adjacent to {{ill|Katayama Park|ja|片山公園}}.<ref>{{cite web | url=https://www.nipponkaigi.osaka/?p=831 | title=【Report】Councilors' Meeting at the Osaka Local Assembly | publisher=Nippon Kaigi Osaka | date=2012-04-01 | accessdate=2024-02-27 }}</ref>
== Grounds == * Main Hall - Rebuilt in 1984 (Shōwa 59). * Worship Hall - Rebuilt in 1984 (Shōwa 59). * Shrine Office
=== Auxiliary and Subordinate Shrines === * {{ill|Toyokawa Inari|ja|豊川稲荷}} Shrine * Jūnishi Inari Daimyōjin - Small shrines dedicated to each of the {{ill|Chinese Zodiac|ja|十二支}} animals. * {{ill|Atago Shrine|ja|愛宕社}} * {{ill|Kamimusubi|ja|神産巣日神}} Shrine
== Festivals == * Autumn Festival: October 19 (Eve) and October 20 (Main Day)
== Access == * A 7-minute walk from {{ill|Suita Station (JR West)|ja|吹田駅 (JR西日本)}} on the {{ill|JR Kyoto Line|ja|JR京都線}} of the {{ill|West Japan Railway Company|ja|西日本旅客鉄道}}. * A 9-minute walk from {{ill|Suita Station (Hankyu)|ja|吹田駅 (阪急)}} on the {{ill|Hankyu Senri Line|ja|阪急千里線}}.
== References == {{reflist}}
== Further Reading == * Shrine History of Katayama Shrine

=== Auxiliary and Subordinate Shrines === * {{ill|Toyokawa Inari|ja|豊川稲荷}} Shrine * Jūnishi Inari Daimyōjin - Small shrines dedicated to each of the {{ill|Chinese Zodiac|ja|十二支}} animals. * {{ill|Atago Shrine|ja|愛宕社}} * {{ill|Kamimusubi|ja|神産巣日神}} Shrine
== Festivals == * Autumn Festival: October 19 (Eve) and October 20 (Main Day)
== Access == * A 7-minute walk from {{ill|Suita Station (JR West)|ja|吹田駅 (JR西日本)}} on the {{ill|JR Kyoto Line|ja|JR京都線}} of the {{ill|West Japan Railway Company|ja|西日本旅客鉄道}}. * A 9-minute walk from {{ill|Suita Station (Hankyu)|ja|吹田駅 (阪急)}} on the {{ill|Hankyu Senri Line|ja|阪急千里線}}.

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Katayama Shrine |location = {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}} {{ill|Suita City|ja|吹田市}} Deguchi-cho 3-3 | LatitudeDegrees = 34 | LatitudeMinutes = 45 | LatitudeSeconds = 54 | N/S = N | LongitudeDegrees = 135 | LongitudeMinutes = 31 | LongitudeSeconds = 6.5 | E/W = E |MapCountryCode = JP |Deities = {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素盞烏尊}}, {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami}}, {{ill|Sumiyoshi Sanjin|ja|住吉三神}} |Foundation = Unknown |ShrineRank = |MainHall = |AnnualFestival = Autumn Festival |Rites = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Katayama jinja.jpg|300px]] |Map = }} '''Katayama Shrine''' (片山神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Suita City|ja|吹田市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}. The formal name of the shrine is "Susanoo Shrine" (素盞嗚尊神社).
== Deities == * Main deity: {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素盞烏尊}} * Associated deities: {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami}}, {{ill|Sumiyoshi Sanjin|ja|住吉三神}}
== History == The year of the shrine's foundation is unknown. It is believed to have originated in the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, when local residents engaged in pottery enshrined {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素盞烏尊}}, the ancestral deity of ceramics.
The shrine was completely destroyed by fire during the {{ill|Tenshō period|ja|天正}} (1573–1592) and again in 1914 (Taishō 3). As a result, old documents detailing its establishment and history were lost, leaving its origins unclear.
The shrine’s chief priest, Yasumi Etō, serves as chairman of the Osaka branch of the {{ill|Nippon Kaigi|ja|日本会議}} and head of the Osaka chapter of the {{ill|Shinto Political Federation|ja|神道政治連盟}}. The shrine is adjacent to {{ill|Katayama Park|ja|片山公園}}.<ref>{{cite web | url=https://www.nipponkaigi.osaka/?p=831 | title=【Report】Councilors' Meeting at the Osaka Local Assembly | publisher=Nippon Kaigi Osaka | date=2012-04-01 | accessdate=2024-02-27 }}</ref>
== Grounds == * Main Hall - Rebuilt in 1984 (Shōwa 59). * Worship Hall - Rebuilt in 1984 (Shōwa 59). * Shrine Office
=== Auxiliary and Subordinate Shrines === * {{ill|Toyokawa Inari|ja|豊川稲荷}} Shrine * Jūnishi Inari Daimyōjin - Small shrines dedicated to each of the {{ill|Chinese Zodiac|ja|十二支}} animals. * {{ill|Atago Shrine|ja|愛宕社}} * {{ill|Kamimusubi|ja|神産巣日神}} Shrine
== Festivals == * Autumn Festival: October 19 (Eve) and October 20 (Main Day)
== Access == * A 7-minute walk from {{ill|Suita Station (JR West)|ja|吹田駅 (JR西日本)}} on the {{ill|JR Kyoto Line|ja|JR京都線}} of the {{ill|West Japan Railway Company|ja|西日本旅客鉄道}}. * A 9-minute walk from {{ill|Suita Station (Hankyu)|ja|吹田駅 (阪急)}} on the {{ill|Hankyu Senri Line|ja|阪急千里線}}.
== References == {{reflist}}
== Further Reading == * Shrine History of Katayama Shrine

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Katayama Shrine |location = {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}} {{ill|Suita City|ja|吹田市}} Deguchi-cho 3-3 | LatitudeDegrees = 34 | LatitudeMinutes = 45 | LatitudeSeconds = 54 | N/S = N | LongitudeDegrees = 135 | LongitudeMinutes = 31 | LongitudeSeconds = 6.5 | E/W = E |MapCountryCode = JP |Deities = {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素盞烏尊}}, {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami}}, {{ill|Sumiyoshi Sanjin|ja|住吉三神}} |Foundation = Unknown |ShrineRank = |MainHall = |AnnualFestival = Autumn Festival |Rites = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Katayama jinja.jpg|300px]] |Map = }} '''Katayama Shrine''' (片山神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Suita City|ja|吹田市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}. The formal name of the shrine is "Susanoo Shrine" (素盞嗚尊神社).
== Deities == * Main deity: {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素盞烏尊}} * Associated deities: {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami}}, {{ill|Sumiyoshi Sanjin|ja|住吉三神}}
== History == The year of the shrine's foundation is unknown. It is believed to have originated in the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, when local residents engaged in pottery enshrined {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素盞烏尊}}, the ancestral deity of ceramics.
The shrine was completely destroyed by fire during the {{ill|Tenshō period|ja|天正}} (1573–1592) and again in 1914 (Taishō 3). As a result, old documents detailing its establishment and history were lost, leaving its origins unclear.
The shrine’s chief priest, Yasumi Etō, serves as chairman of the Osaka branch of the {{ill|Nippon Kaigi|ja|日本会議}} and head of the Osaka chapter of the {{ill|Shinto Political Federation|ja|神道政治連盟}}. The shrine is adjacent to {{ill|Katayama Park|ja|片山公園}}.<ref>{{cite web | url=https://www.nipponkaigi.osaka/?p=831 | title=【Report】Councilors' Meeting at the Osaka Local Assembly | publisher=Nippon Kaigi Osaka | date=2012-04-01 | accessdate=2024-02-27 }}</ref>
== Grounds == * Main Hall - Rebuilt in 1984 (Shōwa 59). * Worship Hall - Rebuilt in 1984 (Shōwa 59). * Shrine Office
=== Auxiliary and Subordinate Shrines === * {{ill|Toyokawa Inari|ja|豊川稲荷}} Shrine * Jūnishi Inari Daimyōjin - Small shrines dedicated to each of the {{ill|Chinese Zodiac|ja|十二支}} animals. * {{ill|Atago Shrine|ja|愛宕社}} * {{ill|Kamimusubi|ja|神産巣日神}} Shrine
== Festivals == * Autumn Festival: October 19 (Eve) and October 20 (Main Day)
== Access == * A 7-minute walk from {{ill|Suita Station (JR West)|ja|吹田駅 (JR西日本)}} on the {{ill|JR Kyoto Line|ja|JR京都線}} of the {{ill|West Japan Railway Company|ja|西日本旅客鉄道}}. * A 9-minute walk from {{ill|Suita Station (Hankyu)|ja|吹田駅 (阪急)}} on the {{ill|Hankyu Senri Line|ja|阪急千里線}}.
== References == {{reflist}}
== Further Reading == * Shrine History of Katayama Shrine

revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Katayama Shrine |location = {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}} {{ill|Suita City|ja|吹田市}} Deguchi-cho 3-3 | LatitudeDegrees = 34 | LatitudeMinutes = 45 | LatitudeSeconds = 54 | N/S = N | LongitudeDegrees = 135 | LongitudeMinutes = 31 | LongitudeSeconds = 6.5 | E/W = E |MapCountryCode = JP |Deities = {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素盞烏尊}}, {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami}}, {{ill|Sumiyoshi Sanjin|ja|住吉三神}} |Foundation = Unknown |ShrineRank = |MainHall = |AnnualFestival = Autumn Festival |Rites = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Katayama jinja.jpg|300px]] |Map = }} '''Katayama Shrine''' (片山神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Suita City|ja|吹田市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}. The formal name of the shrine is "Susanoo Shrine" (素盞嗚尊神社).
== Deities == * Main deity: {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素盞烏尊}} * Associated deities: {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami}}, {{ill|Sumiyoshi Sanjin|ja|住吉三神}}
== History == The year of the shrine's foundation is unknown. It is believed to have originated in the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, when local residents engaged in pottery enshrined {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素盞烏尊}}, the ancestral deity of ceramics.
The shrine was completely destroyed by fire during the {{ill|Tenshō period|ja|天正}} (1573–1592) and again in 1914 (Taishō 3). As a result, old documents detailing its establishment and history were lost, leaving its origins unclear.
The shrine’s chief priest, Yasumi Etō, serves as chairman of the Osaka branch of the {{ill|Nippon Kaigi|ja|日本会議}} and head of the Osaka chapter of the {{ill|Shinto Political Federation|ja|神道政治連盟}}. The shrine is adjacent to {{ill|Katayama Park|ja|片山公園}}.<ref>{{cite web | url=https://www.nipponkaigi.osaka/?p=831 | title=【Report】Councilors' Meeting at the Osaka Local Assembly | publisher=Nippon Kaigi Osaka | date=2012-04-01 | accessdate=2024-02-27 }}</ref>
== Grounds == * Main Hall - Rebuilt in 1984 (Shōwa 59). * Worship Hall - Rebuilt in 1984 (Shōwa 59). * Shrine Office
=== Auxiliary and Subordinate Shrines === * {{ill|Toyokawa Inari|ja|豊川稲荷}} Shrine * Jūnishi Inari Daimyōjin - Small shrines dedicated to each of the {{ill|Chinese Zodiac|ja|十二支}} animals. * {{ill|Atago Shrine|ja|愛宕社}} * {{ill|Kamimusubi|ja|神産巣日神}} Shrine
== Festivals == * Autumn Festival: October 19 (Eve) and October 20 (Main Day)
== Access == * A 7-minute walk from {{ill|Suita Station (JR West)|ja|吹田駅 (JR西日本)}} on the {{ill|JR Kyoto Line|ja|JR京都線}} of the {{ill|West Japan Railway Company|ja|西日本旅客鉄道}}. * A 9-minute walk from {{ill|Suita Station (Hankyu)|ja|吹田駅 (阪急)}} on the {{ill|Hankyu Senri Line|ja|阪急千里線}}.
== References == {{reflist}}
== Further Reading == * Shrine History of Katayama Shrine

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Katayama Shrine |location = {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}} {{ill|Suita City|ja|吹田市}} Deguchi-cho 3-3 | LatitudeDegrees = 34 | LatitudeMinutes = 45 | LatitudeSeconds = 54 | N/S = N | LongitudeDegrees = 135 | LongitudeMinutes = 31 | LongitudeSeconds = 6.5 | E/W = E |MapCountryCode = JP |Deities = {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素盞烏尊}}, {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami}}, {{ill|Sumiyoshi Sanjin|ja|住吉三神}} |Foundation = Unknown |ShrineRank = |MainHall = |AnnualFestival = Autumn Festival |Rites = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Katayama jinja.jpg|300px]] |Map = }} '''Katayama Shrine''' (片山神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Suita City|ja|吹田市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}. The formal name of the shrine is "Susanoo Shrine" (素盞嗚尊神社).
== Deities == * Main deity: {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素盞烏尊}} * Associated deities: {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami}}, {{ill|Sumiyoshi Sanjin|ja|住吉三神}}
== History == The year of the shrine's foundation is unknown. It is believed to have originated in the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, when local residents engaged in pottery enshrined {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素盞烏尊}}, the ancestral deity of ceramics.
The shrine was completely destroyed by fire during the {{ill|Tenshō period|ja|天正}} (1573–1592) and again in 1914 (Taishō 3). As a result, old documents detailing its establishment and history were lost, leaving its origins unclear.
The shrine’s chief priest, Yasumi Etō, serves as chairman of the Osaka branch of the {{ill|Nippon Kaigi|ja|日本会議}} and head of the Osaka chapter of the {{ill|Shinto Political Federation|ja|神道政治連盟}}. The shrine is adjacent to {{ill|Katayama Park|ja|片山公園}}.<ref>{{cite web | url=https://www.nipponkaigi.osaka/?p=831 | title=【Report】Councilors' Meeting at the Osaka Local Assembly | publisher=Nippon Kaigi Osaka | date=2012-04-01 | accessdate=2024-02-27 }}</ref>
== Grounds == * Main Hall - Rebuilt in 1984 (Shōwa 59). * Worship Hall - Rebuilt in 1984 (Shōwa 59). * Shrine Office
=== Auxiliary and Subordinate Shrines === * {{ill|Toyokawa Inari|ja|豊川稲荷}} Shrine * Jūnishi Inari Daimyōjin - Small shrines dedicated to each of the {{ill|Chinese Zodiac|ja|十二支}} animals. * {{ill|Atago Shrine|ja|愛宕社}} * {{ill|Kamimusubi|ja|神産巣日神}} Shrine
== Festivals == * Autumn Festival: October 19 (Eve) and October 20 (Main Day)
== Access == * A 7-minute walk from {{ill|Suita Station (JR West)|ja|吹田駅 (JR西日本)}} on the {{ill|JR Kyoto Line|ja|JR京都線}} of the {{ill|West Japan Railway Company|ja|西日本旅客鉄道}}. * A 9-minute walk from {{ill|Suita Station (Hankyu)|ja|吹田駅 (阪急)}} on the {{ill|Hankyu Senri Line|ja|阪急千里線}}.
== References == {{reflist}}
== Further Reading == * Shrine History of Katayama Shrine

== Access == * A 7-minute walk from {{ill|Suita Station (JR West)|ja|吹田駅 (JR西日本)}} on the {{ill|JR Kyoto Line|ja|JR京都線}} of the {{ill|West Japan Railway Company|ja|西日本旅客鉄道}}. * A 9-minute walk from {{ill|Suita Station (Hankyu)|ja|吹田駅 (阪急)}} on the {{ill|Hankyu Senri Line|ja|阪急千里線}}.
== References == {{reflist}}
== Further Reading == * Shrine History of Katayama Shrine

revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Katayama Shrine |location = {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}} {{ill|Suita City|ja|吹田市}} Deguchi-cho 3-3 | LatitudeDegrees = 34 | LatitudeMinutes = 45 | LatitudeSeconds = 54 | N/S = N | LongitudeDegrees = 135 | LongitudeMinutes = 31 | LongitudeSeconds = 6.5 | E/W = E |MapCountryCode = JP |Deities = {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素盞烏尊}}, {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami}}, {{ill|Sumiyoshi Sanjin|ja|住吉三神}} |Foundation = Unknown |ShrineRank = |MainHall = |AnnualFestival = Autumn Festival |Rites = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Katayama jinja.jpg|300px]] |Map = }} '''Katayama Shrine''' (片山神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Suita City|ja|吹田市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}. The formal name of the shrine is "Susanoo Shrine" (素盞嗚尊神社).
== Deities == * Main deity: {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素盞烏尊}} * Associated deities: {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami}}, {{ill|Sumiyoshi Sanjin|ja|住吉三神}}
== History == The year of the shrine's foundation is unknown. It is believed to have originated in the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, when local residents engaged in pottery enshrined {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素盞烏尊}}, the ancestral deity of ceramics.
The shrine was completely destroyed by fire during the {{ill|Tenshō period|ja|天正}} (1573–1592) and again in 1914 (Taishō 3). As a result, old documents detailing its establishment and history were lost, leaving its origins unclear.
The shrine’s chief priest, Yasumi Etō, serves as chairman of the Osaka branch of the {{ill|Nippon Kaigi|ja|日本会議}} and head of the Osaka chapter of the {{ill|Shinto Political Federation|ja|神道政治連盟}}. The shrine is adjacent to {{ill|Katayama Park|ja|片山公園}}.<ref>{{cite web | url=https://www.nipponkaigi.osaka/?p=831 | title=【Report】Councilors' Meeting at the Osaka Local Assembly | publisher=Nippon Kaigi Osaka | date=2012-04-01 | accessdate=2024-02-27 }}</ref>
== Grounds == * Main Hall - Rebuilt in 1984 (Shōwa 59). * Worship Hall - Rebuilt in 1984 (Shōwa 59). * Shrine Office
=== Auxiliary and Subordinate Shrines === * {{ill|Toyokawa Inari|ja|豊川稲荷}} Shrine * Jūnishi Inari Daimyōjin - Small shrines dedicated to each of the {{ill|Chinese Zodiac|ja|十二支}} animals. * {{ill|Atago Shrine|ja|愛宕社}} * {{ill|Kamimusubi|ja|神産巣日神}} Shrine
== Festivals == * Autumn Festival: October 19 (Eve) and October 20 (Main Day)
== Access == * A 7-minute walk from {{ill|Suita Station (JR West)|ja|吹田駅 (JR西日本)}} on the {{ill|JR Kyoto Line|ja|JR京都線}} of the {{ill|West Japan Railway Company|ja|西日本旅客鉄道}}. * A 9-minute walk from {{ill|Suita Station (Hankyu)|ja|吹田駅 (阪急)}} on the {{ill|Hankyu Senri Line|ja|阪急千里線}}.
== References == {{reflist}}
== Further Reading == * Shrine History of Katayama Shrine

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Katayama Shrine |location = {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}} {{ill|Suita City|ja|吹田市}} Deguchi-cho 3-3 | LatitudeDegrees = 34 | LatitudeMinutes = 45 | LatitudeSeconds = 54 | N/S = N | LongitudeDegrees = 135 | LongitudeMinutes = 31 | LongitudeSeconds = 6.5 | E/W = E |MapCountryCode = JP |Deities = {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素盞烏尊}}, {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami}}, {{ill|Sumiyoshi Sanjin|ja|住吉三神}} |Foundation = Unknown |ShrineRank = |MainHall = |AnnualFestival = Autumn Festival |Rites = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Katayama jinja.jpg|300px]] |Map = }} '''Katayama Shrine''' (片山神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Suita City|ja|吹田市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}. The formal name of the shrine is "Susanoo Shrine" (素盞嗚尊神社).
== Deities == * Main deity: {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素盞烏尊}} * Associated deities: {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami}}, {{ill|Sumiyoshi Sanjin|ja|住吉三神}}
== History == The year of the shrine's foundation is unknown. It is believed to have originated in the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, when local residents engaged in pottery enshrined {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素盞烏尊}}, the ancestral deity of ceramics.
The shrine was completely destroyed by fire during the {{ill|Tenshō period|ja|天正}} (1573–1592) and again in 1914 (Taishō 3). As a result, old documents detailing its establishment and history were lost, leaving its origins unclear.
The shrine’s chief priest, Yasumi Etō, serves as chairman of the Osaka branch of the {{ill|Nippon Kaigi|ja|日本会議}} and head of the Osaka chapter of the {{ill|Shinto Political Federation|ja|神道政治連盟}}. The shrine is adjacent to {{ill|Katayama Park|ja|片山公園}}.<ref>{{cite web | url=https://www.nipponkaigi.osaka/?p=831 | title=【Report】Councilors' Meeting at the Osaka Local Assembly | publisher=Nippon Kaigi Osaka | date=2012-04-01 | accessdate=2024-02-27 }}</ref>
== Grounds == * Main Hall - Rebuilt in 1984 (Shōwa 59). * Worship Hall - Rebuilt in 1984 (Shōwa 59). * Shrine Office
=== Auxiliary and Subordinate Shrines === * {{ill|Toyokawa Inari|ja|豊川稲荷}} Shrine * Jūnishi Inari Daimyōjin - Small shrines dedicated to each of the {{ill|Chinese Zodiac|ja|十二支}} animals. * {{ill|Atago Shrine|ja|愛宕社}} * {{ill|Kamimusubi|ja|神産巣日神}} Shrine
== Festivals == * Autumn Festival: October 19 (Eve) and October 20 (Main Day)
== Access == * A 7-minute walk from {{ill|Suita Station (JR West)|ja|吹田駅 (JR西日本)}} on the {{ill|JR Kyoto Line|ja|JR京都線}} of the {{ill|West Japan Railway Company|ja|西日本旅客鉄道}}. * A 9-minute walk from {{ill|Suita Station (Hankyu)|ja|吹田駅 (阪急)}} on the {{ill|Hankyu Senri Line|ja|阪急千里線}}.
== References == {{reflist}}
== Further Reading == * Shrine History of Katayama Shrine

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Katayama Shrine |location = {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}} {{ill|Suita City|ja|吹田市}} Deguchi-cho 3-3 | LatitudeDegrees = 34 | LatitudeMinutes = 45 | LatitudeSeconds = 54 | N/S = N | LongitudeDegrees = 135 | LongitudeMinutes = 31 | LongitudeSeconds = 6.5 | E/W = E |MapCountryCode = JP |Deities = {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素盞烏尊}}, {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami}}, {{ill|Sumiyoshi Sanjin|ja|住吉三神}} |Foundation = Unknown |ShrineRank = |MainHall = |AnnualFestival = Autumn Festival |Rites = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Katayama jinja.jpg|300px]] |Map = }} '''Katayama Shrine''' (片山神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Suita City|ja|吹田市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}. The formal name of the shrine is "Susanoo Shrine" (素盞嗚尊神社).
== Deities == * Main deity: {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素盞烏尊}} * Associated deities: {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami}}, {{ill|Sumiyoshi Sanjin|ja|住吉三神}}
== History == The year of the shrine's foundation is unknown. It is believed to have originated in the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, when local residents engaged in pottery enshrined {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素盞烏尊}}, the ancestral deity of ceramics.
The shrine was completely destroyed by fire during the {{ill|Tenshō period|ja|天正}} (1573–1592) and again in 1914 (Taishō 3). As a result, old documents detailing its establishment and history were lost, leaving its origins unclear.
The shrine’s chief priest, Yasumi Etō, serves as chairman of the Osaka branch of the {{ill|Nippon Kaigi|ja|日本会議}} and head of the Osaka chapter of the {{ill|Shinto Political Federation|ja|神道政治連盟}}. The shrine is adjacent to {{ill|Katayama Park|ja|片山公園}}.<ref>{{cite web | url=https://www.nipponkaigi.osaka/?p=831 | title=【Report】Councilors' Meeting at the Osaka Local Assembly | publisher=Nippon Kaigi Osaka | date=2012-04-01 | accessdate=2024-02-27 }}</ref>
== Grounds == * Main Hall - Rebuilt in 1984 (Shōwa 59). * Worship Hall - Rebuilt in 1984 (Shōwa 59). * Shrine Office
=== Auxiliary and Subordinate Shrines === * {{ill|Toyokawa Inari|ja|豊川稲荷}} Shrine * Jūnishi Inari Daimyōjin - Small shrines dedicated to each of the {{ill|Chinese Zodiac|ja|十二支}} animals. * {{ill|Atago Shrine|ja|愛宕社}} * {{ill|Kamimusubi|ja|神産巣日神}} Shrine
== Festivals == * Autumn Festival: October 19 (Eve) and October 20 (Main Day)
== Access == * A 7-minute walk from {{ill|Suita Station (JR West)|ja|吹田駅 (JR西日本)}} on the {{ill|JR Kyoto Line|ja|JR京都線}} of the {{ill|West Japan Railway Company|ja|西日本旅客鉄道}}. * A 9-minute walk from {{ill|Suita Station (Hankyu)|ja|吹田駅 (阪急)}} on the {{ill|Hankyu Senri Line|ja|阪急千里線}}.
== References == {{reflist}}
== Further Reading == * Shrine History of Katayama Shrine

The shrine was completely destroyed by fire during the {{ill|Tenshō period|ja|天正}} (1573–1592) and again in 1914 (Taishō 3). As a result, old documents detailing its establishment and history were lost, leaving its origins unclear.
The shrine’s chief priest, Yasumi Etō, serves as chairman of the Osaka branch of the {{ill|Nippon Kaigi|ja|日本会議}} and head of the Osaka chapter of the {{ill|Shinto Political Federation|ja|神道政治連盟}}. The shrine is adjacent to {{ill|Katayama Park|ja|片山公園}}.<ref>{{cite web | url=https://www.nipponkaigi.osaka/?p=831 | title=【Report】Councilors' Meeting at the Osaka Local Assembly | publisher=Nippon Kaigi Osaka | date=2012-04-01 | accessdate=2024-02-27 }}</ref>
== Grounds == * Main Hall - Rebuilt in 1984 (Shōwa 59). * Worship Hall - Rebuilt in 1984 (Shōwa 59). * Shrine Office
=== Auxiliary and Subordinate Shrines === * {{ill|Toyokawa Inari|ja|豊川稲荷}} Shrine * Jūnishi Inari Daimyōjin - Small shrines dedicated to each of the {{ill|Chinese Zodiac|ja|十二支}} animals. * {{ill|Atago Shrine|ja|愛宕社}} * {{ill|Kamimusubi|ja|神産巣日神}} Shrine
== Festivals == * Autumn Festival: October 19 (Eve) and October 20 (Main Day)
== Access == * A 7-minute walk from {{ill|Suita Station (JR West)|ja|吹田駅 (JR西日本)}} on the {{ill|JR Kyoto Line|ja|JR京都線}} of the {{ill|West Japan Railway Company|ja|西日本旅客鉄道}}. * A 9-minute walk from {{ill|Suita Station (Hankyu)|ja|吹田駅 (阪急)}} on the {{ill|Hankyu Senri Line|ja|阪急千里線}}.
== References == {{reflist}}
== Further Reading == * Shrine History of Katayama Shrine

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Katayama Shrine |location = {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}} {{ill|Suita City|ja|吹田市}} Deguchi-cho 3-3 | LatitudeDegrees = 34 | LatitudeMinutes = 45 | LatitudeSeconds = 54 | N/S = N | LongitudeDegrees = 135 | LongitudeMinutes = 31 | LongitudeSeconds = 6.5 | E/W = E |MapCountryCode = JP |Deities = {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素盞烏尊}}, {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami}}, {{ill|Sumiyoshi Sanjin|ja|住吉三神}} |Foundation = Unknown |ShrineRank = |MainHall = |AnnualFestival = Autumn Festival |Rites = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Katayama jinja.jpg|300px]] |Map = }} '''Katayama Shrine''' (片山神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Suita City|ja|吹田市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}. The formal name of the shrine is "Susanoo Shrine" (素盞嗚尊神社).
== Deities == * Main deity: {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素盞烏尊}} * Associated deities: {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami}}, {{ill|Sumiyoshi Sanjin|ja|住吉三神}}
== History == The year of the shrine's foundation is unknown. It is believed to have originated in the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, when local residents engaged in pottery enshrined {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素盞烏尊}}, the ancestral deity of ceramics.
The shrine was completely destroyed by fire during the {{ill|Tenshō period|ja|天正}} (1573–1592) and again in 1914 (Taishō 3). As a result, old documents detailing its establishment and history were lost, leaving its origins unclear.
The shrine’s chief priest, Yasumi Etō, serves as chairman of the Osaka branch of the {{ill|Nippon Kaigi|ja|日本会議}} and head of the Osaka chapter of the {{ill|Shinto Political Federation|ja|神道政治連盟}}. The shrine is adjacent to {{ill|Katayama Park|ja|片山公園}}.<ref>{{cite web | url=https://www.nipponkaigi.osaka/?p=831 | title=【Report】Councilors' Meeting at the Osaka Local Assembly | publisher=Nippon Kaigi Osaka | date=2012-04-01 | accessdate=2024-02-27 }}</ref>
== Grounds == * Main Hall - Rebuilt in 1984 (Shōwa 59). * Worship Hall - Rebuilt in 1984 (Shōwa 59). * Shrine Office
=== Auxiliary and Subordinate Shrines === * {{ill|Toyokawa Inari|ja|豊川稲荷}} Shrine * Jūnishi Inari Daimyōjin - Small shrines dedicated to each of the {{ill|Chinese Zodiac|ja|十二支}} animals. * {{ill|Atago Shrine|ja|愛宕社}} * {{ill|Kamimusubi|ja|神産巣日神}} Shrine
== Festivals == * Autumn Festival: October 19 (Eve) and October 20 (Main Day)
== Access == * A 7-minute walk from {{ill|Suita Station (JR West)|ja|吹田駅 (JR西日本)}} on the {{ill|JR Kyoto Line|ja|JR京都線}} of the {{ill|West Japan Railway Company|ja|西日本旅客鉄道}}. * A 9-minute walk from {{ill|Suita Station (Hankyu)|ja|吹田駅 (阪急)}} on the {{ill|Hankyu Senri Line|ja|阪急千里線}}.
== References == {{reflist}}
== Further Reading == * Shrine History of Katayama Shrine

revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Katayama Shrine |location = {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}} {{ill|Suita City|ja|吹田市}} Deguchi-cho 3-3 | LatitudeDegrees = 34 | LatitudeMinutes = 45 | LatitudeSeconds = 54 | N/S = N | LongitudeDegrees = 135 | LongitudeMinutes = 31 | LongitudeSeconds = 6.5 | E/W = E |MapCountryCode = JP |Deities = {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素盞烏尊}}, {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami}}, {{ill|Sumiyoshi Sanjin|ja|住吉三神}} |Foundation = Unknown |ShrineRank = |MainHall = |AnnualFestival = Autumn Festival |Rites = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Katayama jinja.jpg|300px]] |Map = }} '''Katayama Shrine''' (片山神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Suita City|ja|吹田市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}. The formal name of the shrine is "Susanoo Shrine" (素盞嗚尊神社).
== Deities == * Main deity: {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素盞烏尊}} * Associated deities: {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami}}, {{ill|Sumiyoshi Sanjin|ja|住吉三神}}
== History == The year of the shrine's foundation is unknown. It is believed to have originated in the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, when local residents engaged in pottery enshrined {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素盞烏尊}}, the ancestral deity of ceramics.
The shrine was completely destroyed by fire during the {{ill|Tenshō period|ja|天正}} (1573–1592) and again in 1914 (Taishō 3). As a result, old documents detailing its establishment and history were lost, leaving its origins unclear.
The shrine’s chief priest, Yasumi Etō, serves as chairman of the Osaka branch of the {{ill|Nippon Kaigi|ja|日本会議}} and head of the Osaka chapter of the {{ill|Shinto Political Federation|ja|神道政治連盟}}. The shrine is adjacent to {{ill|Katayama Park|ja|片山公園}}.<ref>{{cite web | url=https://www.nipponkaigi.osaka/?p=831 | title=【Report】Councilors' Meeting at the Osaka Local Assembly | publisher=Nippon Kaigi Osaka | date=2012-04-01 | accessdate=2024-02-27 }}</ref>
== Grounds == * Main Hall - Rebuilt in 1984 (Shōwa 59). * Worship Hall - Rebuilt in 1984 (Shōwa 59). * Shrine Office
=== Auxiliary and Subordinate Shrines === * {{ill|Toyokawa Inari|ja|豊川稲荷}} Shrine * Jūnishi Inari Daimyōjin - Small shrines dedicated to each of the {{ill|Chinese Zodiac|ja|十二支}} animals. * {{ill|Atago Shrine|ja|愛宕社}} * {{ill|Kamimusubi|ja|神産巣日神}} Shrine
== Festivals == * Autumn Festival: October 19 (Eve) and October 20 (Main Day)
== Access == * A 7-minute walk from {{ill|Suita Station (JR West)|ja|吹田駅 (JR西日本)}} on the {{ill|JR Kyoto Line|ja|JR京都線}} of the {{ill|West Japan Railway Company|ja|西日本旅客鉄道}}. * A 9-minute walk from {{ill|Suita Station (Hankyu)|ja|吹田駅 (阪急)}} on the {{ill|Hankyu Senri Line|ja|阪急千里線}}.
== References == {{reflist}}
== Further Reading == * Shrine History of Katayama Shrine

revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Katayama Shrine |location = {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}} {{ill|Suita City|ja|吹田市}} Deguchi-cho 3-3 | LatitudeDegrees = 34 | LatitudeMinutes = 45 | LatitudeSeconds = 54 | N/S = N | LongitudeDegrees = 135 | LongitudeMinutes = 31 | LongitudeSeconds = 6.5 | E/W = E |MapCountryCode = JP |Deities = {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素盞烏尊}}, {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami}}, {{ill|Sumiyoshi Sanjin|ja|住吉三神}} |Foundation = Unknown |ShrineRank = |MainHall = |AnnualFestival = Autumn Festival |Rites = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Katayama jinja.jpg|300px]] |Map = }} '''Katayama Shrine''' (片山神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Suita City|ja|吹田市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}. The formal name of the shrine is "Susanoo Shrine" (素盞嗚尊神社).
== Deities == * Main deity: {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素盞烏尊}} * Associated deities: {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami}}, {{ill|Sumiyoshi Sanjin|ja|住吉三神}}
== History == The year of the shrine's foundation is unknown. It is believed to have originated in the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, when local residents engaged in pottery enshrined {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素盞烏尊}}, the ancestral deity of ceramics.
The shrine was completely destroyed by fire during the {{ill|Tenshō period|ja|天正}} (1573–1592) and again in 1914 (Taishō 3). As a result, old documents detailing its establishment and history were lost, leaving its origins unclear.
The shrine’s chief priest, Yasumi Etō, serves as chairman of the Osaka branch of the {{ill|Nippon Kaigi|ja|日本会議}} and head of the Osaka chapter of the {{ill|Shinto Political Federation|ja|神道政治連盟}}. The shrine is adjacent to {{ill|Katayama Park|ja|片山公園}}.<ref>{{cite web | url=https://www.nipponkaigi.osaka/?p=831 | title=【Report】Councilors' Meeting at the Osaka Local Assembly | publisher=Nippon Kaigi Osaka | date=2012-04-01 | accessdate=2024-02-27 }}</ref>
== Grounds == * Main Hall - Rebuilt in 1984 (Shōwa 59). * Worship Hall - Rebuilt in 1984 (Shōwa 59). * Shrine Office
=== Auxiliary and Subordinate Shrines === * {{ill|Toyokawa Inari|ja|豊川稲荷}} Shrine * Jūnishi Inari Daimyōjin - Small shrines dedicated to each of the {{ill|Chinese Zodiac|ja|十二支}} animals. * {{ill|Atago Shrine|ja|愛宕社}} * {{ill|Kamimusubi|ja|神産巣日神}} Shrine
== Festivals == * Autumn Festival: October 19 (Eve) and October 20 (Main Day)
== Access == * A 7-minute walk from {{ill|Suita Station (JR West)|ja|吹田駅 (JR西日本)}} on the {{ill|JR Kyoto Line|ja|JR京都線}} of the {{ill|West Japan Railway Company|ja|西日本旅客鉄道}}. * A 9-minute walk from {{ill|Suita Station (Hankyu)|ja|吹田駅 (阪急)}} on the {{ill|Hankyu Senri Line|ja|阪急千里線}}.
== References == {{reflist}}
== Further Reading == * Shrine History of Katayama Shrine

== Festivals == * Autumn Festival: October 19 (Eve) and October 20 (Main Day)
== Access == * A 7-minute walk from {{ill|Suita Station (JR West)|ja|吹田駅 (JR西日本)}} on the {{ill|JR Kyoto Line|ja|JR京都線}} of the {{ill|West Japan Railway Company|ja|西日本旅客鉄道}}. * A 9-minute walk from {{ill|Suita Station (Hankyu)|ja|吹田駅 (阪急)}} on the {{ill|Hankyu Senri Line|ja|阪急千里線}}.
== References == {{reflist}}
== Further Reading == * Shrine History of Katayama Shrine

revision.comment: Text replacement - "|神産巣日神}}" to "|神産巣日神|1=Kamimusubi|lt=Kamimusubi-no-Kami}}"
revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Katayama Shrine |location = {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}} {{ill|Suita City|ja|吹田市}} Deguchi-cho 3-3 | LatitudeDegrees = 34 | LatitudeMinutes = 45 | LatitudeSeconds = 54 | N/S = N | LongitudeDegrees = 135 | LongitudeMinutes = 31 | LongitudeSeconds = 6.5 | E/W = E |MapCountryCode = JP |Deities = {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素盞烏尊}}, {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami}}, {{ill|Sumiyoshi Sanjin|ja|住吉三神}} |Foundation = Unknown |ShrineRank = |MainHall = |AnnualFestival = Autumn Festival |Rites = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Katayama jinja.jpg|300px]] |Map = }} '''Katayama Shrine''' (片山神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Suita City|ja|吹田市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}. The formal name of the shrine is "Susanoo Shrine" (素盞嗚尊神社).
== Deities == * Main deity: {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素盞烏尊}} * Associated deities: {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami}}, {{ill|Sumiyoshi Sanjin|ja|住吉三神}}
== History == The year of the shrine's foundation is unknown. It is believed to have originated in the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, when local residents engaged in pottery enshrined {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素盞烏尊}}, the ancestral deity of ceramics.
The shrine was completely destroyed by fire during the {{ill|Tenshō period|ja|天正}} (1573–1592) and again in 1914 (Taishō 3). As a result, old documents detailing its establishment and history were lost, leaving its origins unclear.
The shrine’s chief priest, Yasumi Etō, serves as chairman of the Osaka branch of the {{ill|Nippon Kaigi|ja|日本会議}} and head of the Osaka chapter of the {{ill|Shinto Political Federation|ja|神道政治連盟}}. The shrine is adjacent to {{ill|Katayama Park|ja|片山公園}}.<ref>{{cite web | url=https://www.nipponkaigi.osaka/?p=831 | title=【Report】Councilors' Meeting at the Osaka Local Assembly | publisher=Nippon Kaigi Osaka | date=2012-04-01 | accessdate=2024-02-27 }}</ref>
== Grounds == * Main Hall - Rebuilt in 1984 (Shōwa 59). * Worship Hall - Rebuilt in 1984 (Shōwa 59). * Shrine Office
=== Auxiliary and Subordinate Shrines === * {{ill|Toyokawa Inari|ja|豊川稲荷}} Shrine * Jūnishi Inari Daimyōjin - Small shrines dedicated to each of the {{ill|Chinese Zodiac|ja|十二支}} animals. * {{ill|Atago Shrine|ja|愛宕社}} * {{ill|Kamimusubi|ja|神産巣日神|1=Kamimusubi|lt=Kamimusubi-no-Kami}} Shrine
== Festivals == * Autumn Festival: October 19 (Eve) and October 20 (Main Day)
== Access == * A 7-minute walk from {{ill|Suita Station (JR West)|ja|吹田駅 (JR西日本)}} on the {{ill|JR Kyoto Line|ja|JR京都線}} of the {{ill|West Japan Railway Company|ja|西日本旅客鉄道}}. * A 9-minute walk from {{ill|Suita Station (Hankyu)|ja|吹田駅 (阪急)}} on the {{ill|Hankyu Senri Line|ja|阪急千里線}}.
== References == {{reflist}}
== Further Reading == * Shrine History of Katayama Shrine

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Katayama Shrine |location = {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}} {{ill|Suita City|ja|吹田市}} Deguchi-cho 3-3 | LatitudeDegrees = 34 | LatitudeMinutes = 45 | LatitudeSeconds = 54 | N/S = N | LongitudeDegrees = 135 | LongitudeMinutes = 31 | LongitudeSeconds = 6.5 | E/W = E |MapCountryCode = JP |Deities = {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素盞烏尊}}, {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami}}, {{ill|Sumiyoshi Sanjin|ja|住吉三神|1=Sumiyoshi sanjin}} |Foundation = Unknown |ShrineRank = |MainHall = |AnnualFestival = Autumn Festival |Rites = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Katayama jinja.jpg|300px]] |Map = }} '''Katayama Shrine''' (片山神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Suita City|ja|吹田市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}. The formal name of the shrine is "Susanoo Shrine" (素盞嗚尊神社).
== Deities == * Main deity: {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素盞烏尊}} * Associated deities: {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami}}, {{ill|Sumiyoshi Sanjin|ja|住吉三神|1=Sumiyoshi sanjin}}
== History == The year of the shrine's foundation is unknown. It is believed to have originated in the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, when local residents engaged in pottery enshrined {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素盞烏尊}}, the ancestral deity of ceramics.
The shrine was completely destroyed by fire during the {{ill|Tenshō period|ja|天正}} (1573–1592) and again in 1914 (Taishō 3). As a result, old documents detailing its establishment and history were lost, leaving its origins unclear.
The shrine’s chief priest, Yasumi Etō, serves as chairman of the Osaka branch of the {{ill|Nippon Kaigi|ja|日本会議}} and head of the Osaka chapter of the {{ill|Shinto Political Federation|ja|神道政治連盟}}. The shrine is adjacent to {{ill|Katayama Park|ja|片山公園}}.<ref>{{cite web | url=https://www.nipponkaigi.osaka/?p=831 | title=【Report】Councilors' Meeting at the Osaka Local Assembly | publisher=Nippon Kaigi Osaka | date=2012-04-01 | accessdate=2024-02-27 }}</ref>
== Grounds == * Main Hall - Rebuilt in 1984 (Shōwa 59). * Worship Hall - Rebuilt in 1984 (Shōwa 59). * Shrine Office
=== Auxiliary and Subordinate Shrines === * {{ill|Toyokawa Inari|ja|豊川稲荷}} Shrine * Jūnishi Inari Daimyōjin - Small shrines dedicated to each of the {{ill|Chinese Zodiac|ja|十二支}} animals. * {{ill|Atago Shrine|ja|愛宕社}} * {{ill|Kamimusubi|ja|神産巣日神|1=Kamimusubi|lt=Kamimusubi-no-Kami}} Shrine
== Festivals == * Autumn Festival: October 19 (Eve) and October 20 (Main Day)
== Access == * A 7-minute walk from {{ill|Suita Station (JR West)|ja|吹田駅 (JR西日本)}} on the {{ill|JR Kyoto Line|ja|JR京都線}} of the {{ill|West Japan Railway Company|ja|西日本旅客鉄道}}. * A 9-minute walk from {{ill|Suita Station (Hankyu)|ja|吹田駅 (阪急)}} on the {{ill|Hankyu Senri Line|ja|阪急千里線}}.
== References == {{reflist}}
== Further Reading == * Shrine History of Katayama Shrine

The formal name of the shrine is "Susanoo Shrine" (素盞嗚尊神社).

== History == The year of the shrine's foundation is unknown. It is believed to have originated in the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, when local residents engaged in pottery enshrined {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素盞烏尊}}, the ancestral deity of ceramics.
The shrine was completely destroyed by fire during the {{ill|Tenshō period|ja|天正|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)|lt=Tenshō Era}} (1573–1592) and again in 1914 (Taishō 3). As a result, old documents detailing its establishment and history were lost, leaving its origins unclear.

The shrine is adjacent to {{ill|Katayama Park|ja|片山公園}}.<ref>{{cite web | url=https://www.nipponkaigi.osaka/?p=831 | title=【Report】Councilors' Meeting at the Osaka Local Assembly | publisher=Nippon Kaigi Osaka | date=2012-04-01 | accessdate=2024-02-27 }}</ref>

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Katayama Shrine |location = {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}} {{ill|Suita City|ja|吹田市}} Deguchi-cho 3-3 | LatitudeDegrees = 34 | LatitudeMinutes = 45 | LatitudeSeconds = 54 | N/S = N | LongitudeDegrees = 135 | LongitudeMinutes = 31 | LongitudeSeconds = 6.5 | E/W = E |MapCountryCode = JP |Deities = {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素盞烏尊}}, {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami}}, {{ill|Sumiyoshi Sanjin|ja|住吉三神|1=Sumiyoshi sanjin}} |Foundation = Unknown |ShrineRank = |MainHall = |AnnualFestival = Autumn Festival |Rites = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Katayama jinja.jpg|300px]] |Map = }} '''Katayama Shrine''' (片山神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Suita City|ja|吹田市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}. The formal name of the shrine is "Susanoo Shrine" (素盞嗚尊神社).
== Deities == * Main deity: {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素盞烏尊}} * Associated deities: {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami}}, {{ill|Sumiyoshi Sanjin|ja|住吉三神|1=Sumiyoshi sanjin}}
== History == The year of the shrine's foundation is unknown. It is believed to have originated in the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, when local residents engaged in pottery enshrined {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素盞烏尊}}, the ancestral deity of ceramics.
The shrine was completely destroyed by fire during the {{ill|Tenshō period|ja|天正|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)|lt=Tenshō Era}} (1573–1592) and again in 1914 (Taishō 3). As a result, old documents detailing its establishment and history were lost, leaving its origins unclear.
The shrine’s chief priest, Yasumi Etō, serves as chairman of the Osaka branch of the {{ill|Nippon Kaigi|ja|日本会議}} and head of the Osaka chapter of the {{ill|Shinto Political Federation|ja|神道政治連盟}}. The shrine is adjacent to {{ill|Katayama Park|ja|片山公園}}.<ref>{{cite web | url=https://www.nipponkaigi.osaka/?p=831 | title=【Report】Councilors' Meeting at the Osaka Local Assembly | publisher=Nippon Kaigi Osaka | date=2012-04-01 | accessdate=2024-02-27 }}</ref>
== Grounds == * Main Hall - Rebuilt in 1984 (Shōwa 59). * Worship Hall - Rebuilt in 1984 (Shōwa 59). * Shrine Office
=== Auxiliary and Subordinate Shrines === * {{ill|Toyokawa Inari|ja|豊川稲荷}} Shrine * Jūnishi Inari Daimyōjin - Small shrines dedicated to each of the {{ill|Chinese Zodiac|ja|十二支}} animals. * {{ill|Atago Shrine|ja|愛宕社}} * {{ill|Kamimusubi|ja|神産巣日神|1=Kamimusubi|lt=Kamimusubi-no-Kami}} Shrine
== Festivals == * Autumn Festival: October 19 (Eve) and October 20 (Main Day)
== Access == * A 7-minute walk from {{ill|Suita Station (JR West)|ja|吹田駅 (JR西日本)}} on the {{ill|JR Kyoto Line|ja|JR京都線}} of the {{ill|West Japan Railway Company|ja|西日本旅客鉄道}}. * A 9-minute walk from {{ill|Suita Station (Hankyu)|ja|吹田駅 (阪急)}} on the {{ill|Hankyu Senri Line|ja|阪急千里線}}.
== References == {{reflist}}
== Further Reading == * Shrine History of Katayama Shrine

== History == The year of the shrine's foundation is unknown. It is believed to have originated in the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, when local residents engaged in pottery enshrined {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素盞烏尊}}, the ancestral deity of ceramics.
The shrine was completely destroyed by fire during the {{ill|Tenshō period|ja|天正|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)|lt=Tenshō Era}} (1573–1592) and again in 1914 (Taishō 3). As a result, old documents detailing its establishment and history were lost, leaving its origins unclear.

The shrine is adjacent to {{ill|Katayama Park|ja|片山公園}}.<ref>{{cite web | url=https://www.nipponkaigi.osaka/?p=831 | title=【Report】Councilors' Meeting at the Osaka Local Assembly | publisher=Nippon Kaigi Osaka | date=2012-04-01 | accessdate=2024-02-27 }}</ref>

The formal name of the shrine is "Susanoo Shrine" (素盞嗚尊神社).
== Deities == * Main deity: {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素盞烏尊}} * Associated deities: {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami}}, {{ill|Sumiyoshi Sanjin|ja|住吉三神|1=Sumiyoshi sanjin}}
== History == The year of the shrine's foundation is unknown. It is believed to have originated in the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, when local residents engaged in pottery enshrined {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素盞烏尊}}, the ancestral deity of ceramics.
The shrine was completely destroyed by fire during the {{ill|Tenshō period|ja|天正|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)|lt=Tenshō Era}} (1573–1592) and again in 1914 (Taishō 3). As a result, old documents detailing its establishment and history were lost, leaving its origins unclear.
The shrine’s chief priest, Yasumi Etō, serves as chairman of the Osaka branch of the {{ill|Nippon Kaigi|ja|日本会議}} and head of the Osaka chapter of the {{ill|Shinto Political Federation|ja|神道政治連盟}}. The shrine is adjacent to {{ill|Katayama Park|ja|片山公園}}.<ref>{{cite web | url=https://www.nipponkaigi.osaka/?p=831 | title=【Report】Councilors' Meeting at the Osaka Local Assembly | publisher=Nippon Kaigi Osaka | date=2012-04-01 | accessdate=2024-02-27 }}</ref>
== Grounds == * Main Hall - Rebuilt in 1984 (Shōwa 59). * Worship Hall - Rebuilt in 1984 (Shōwa 59). * Shrine Office
=== Auxiliary and Subordinate Shrines === * {{ill|Toyokawa Inari|ja|豊川稲荷}} Shrine * Jūnishi Inari Daimyōjin - Small shrines dedicated to each of the {{ill|Chinese Zodiac|ja|十二支}} animals. * {{ill|Atago Shrine|ja|愛宕社}} * {{ill|Kamimusubi|ja|神産巣日神|1=Kamimusubi|lt=Kamimusubi-no-Kami}} Shrine
== Festivals == * Autumn Festival: October 19 (Eve) and October 20 (Main Day)
== Access == * A 7-minute walk from {{ill|Suita Station (JR West)|ja|吹田駅 (JR西日本)}} on the {{ill|JR Kyoto Line|ja|JR京都線}} of the {{ill|West Japan Railway Company|ja|西日本旅客鉄道}}. * A 9-minute walk from {{ill|Suita Station (Hankyu)|ja|吹田駅 (阪急)}} on the {{ill|Hankyu Senri Line|ja|阪急千里線}}.
== References == {{reflist}}
== Further Reading == * Shrine History of Katayama Shrine

== History == The year of the shrine's foundation is unknown. It is believed to have originated in the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, when local residents engaged in pottery enshrined {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素盞烏尊}}, the ancestral deity of ceramics.
The shrine was completely destroyed by fire during the {{ill|Tenshō period|ja|天正|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)|lt=Tenshō Era}} (1573–1592) and again in 1914 (Taishō 3). As a result, old documents detailing its establishment and history were lost, leaving its origins unclear.
The shrine’s chief priest, Yasumi Etō, serves as chairman of the Osaka branch of the {{ill|Nippon Kaigi|ja|日本会議}} and head of the Osaka chapter of the {{ill|Shinto Political Federation|ja|神道政治連盟}}. The shrine is adjacent to {{ill|Katayama Park|ja|片山公園}}.<ref>{{cite web | url=https://www.nipponkaigi.osaka/?p=831 | title=【Report】Councilors' Meeting at the Osaka Local Assembly | publisher=Nippon Kaigi Osaka | date=2012-04-01 | accessdate=2024-02-27 }}</ref>
