revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Katayama Shrine |location = {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}} {{ill|Suita City|ja|吹田市}} Deguchi-cho 3-3 | LatitudeDegrees = 34 | LatitudeMinutes = 45 | LatitudeSeconds = 54 | N/S = N | LongitudeDegrees = 135 | LongitudeMinutes = 31 | LongitudeSeconds = 6.5 | E/W = E |MapCountryCode = JP |Deities = {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素盞烏尊}}, {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami}}, {{ill|Sumiyoshi Sanjin|ja|住吉三神|1=Sumiyoshi sanjin}} |Foundation = Unknown |ShrineRank = |MainHall = |AnnualFestival = Autumn Festival |Rites = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Katayama jinja.jpg|300px]] |Map = }} '''Katayama Shrine''' (片山神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Suita City|ja|吹田市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}. The formal name of the shrine is "Susanoo Shrine" (素盞嗚尊神社).
== Deities == * Main deity: {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素盞烏尊}} * Associated deities: {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami}}, {{ill|Sumiyoshi Sanjin|ja|住吉三神|1=Sumiyoshi sanjin}}
== History == The year of the shrine's foundation is unknown. It is believed to have originated in the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, when local residents engaged in pottery enshrined {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素盞烏尊}}, the ancestral deity of ceramics.
The shrine was completely destroyed by fire during the {{ill|Tenshō period|ja|天正|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)|lt=Tenshō Era}} (1573–1592) and again in 1914 (Taishō 3). As a result, old documents detailing its establishment and history were lost, leaving its origins unclear.
The shrine’s chief priest, Yasumi Etō, serves as chairman of the Osaka branch of the {{ill|Nippon Kaigi|ja|日本会議}} and head of the Osaka chapter of the {{ill|Shinto Political Federation|ja|神道政治連盟}}. The shrine is adjacent to {{ill|Katayama Park|ja|片山公園}}.<ref>{{cite web | url=https://www.nipponkaigi.osaka/?p=831 | title=【Report】Councilors' Meeting at the Osaka Local Assembly | publisher=Nippon Kaigi Osaka | date=2012-04-01 | accessdate=2024-02-27 }}</ref>
== Grounds == * Main Hall - Rebuilt in 1984 (Shōwa 59). * Worship Hall - Rebuilt in 1984 (Shōwa 59). * Shrine Office
=== Auxiliary and Subordinate Shrines === * {{ill|Toyokawa Inari|ja|豊川稲荷}} Shrine * Jūnishi Inari Daimyōjin - Small shrines dedicated to each of the {{ill|Chinese Zodiac|ja|十二支}} animals. * {{ill|Atago Shrine|ja|愛宕社}} * {{ill|Kamimusubi|ja|神産巣日神|1=Kamimusubi|lt=Kamimusubi-no-Kami}} Shrine
== Festivals == * Autumn Festival: October 19 (Eve) and October 20 (Main Day)
== Access == * A 7-minute walk from {{ill|Suita Station (JR West)|ja|吹田駅 (JR西日本)}} on the {{ill|JR Kyoto Line|ja|JR京都線}} of the {{ill|West Japan Railway Company|ja|西日本旅客鉄道}}. * A 9-minute walk from {{ill|Suita Station (Hankyu)|ja|吹田駅 (阪急)}} on the {{ill|Hankyu Senri Line|ja|阪急千里線}}.
== References == {{reflist}}
== Further Reading == * Shrine History of Katayama Shrine

== Access == * A 7-minute walk from {{ill|Suita Station (JR West)|ja|吹田駅 (JR西日本)|12=draft}} on the {{ill|JR Kyoto Line|ja|JR京都線|12=draft}} of the {{ill|West Japan Railway Company|ja|西日本旅客鉄道|12=draft}}. * A 9-minute walk from {{ill|Suita Station (Hankyu)|ja|吹田駅 (阪急)|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Hankyu Senri Line|ja|阪急千里線|12=draft}}.

revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Katayama Shrine |location = {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府|12=draft}} {{ill|Suita City|ja|吹田市|12=draft}} Deguchi-cho 3-3 | LatitudeDegrees = 34 | LatitudeMinutes = 45 | LatitudeSeconds = 54 | N/S = N | LongitudeDegrees = 135 | LongitudeMinutes = 31 | LongitudeSeconds = 6.5 | E/W = E |MapCountryCode = JP |Deities = {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素盞烏尊|12=draft}}, {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|12=draft}}, {{ill|Sumiyoshi Sanjin|ja|住吉三神|1=Sumiyoshi sanjin|12=draft}} |Foundation = Unknown |ShrineRank = |MainHall = |AnnualFestival = Autumn Festival |Rites = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Katayama jinja.jpg|300px]] |Map = }} '''Katayama Shrine''' (片山神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Suita City|ja|吹田市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府|12=draft}}. The formal name of the shrine is "Susanoo Shrine" (素盞嗚尊神社).
== Deities == * Main deity: {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素盞烏尊|12=draft}} * Associated deities: {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|12=draft}}, {{ill|Sumiyoshi Sanjin|ja|住吉三神|1=Sumiyoshi sanjin|12=draft}}
== History == The year of the shrine's foundation is unknown. It is believed to have originated in the {{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代|12=draft}}, when local residents engaged in pottery enshrined {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素盞烏尊|12=draft}}, the ancestral deity of ceramics.
The shrine was completely destroyed by fire during the {{ill|Tenshō period|ja|天正|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)|lt=Tenshō Era|12=draft}} (1573–1592) and again in 1914 (Taishō 3). As a result, old documents detailing its establishment and history were lost, leaving its origins unclear.
The shrine’s chief priest, Yasumi Etō, serves as chairman of the Osaka branch of the {{ill|Nippon Kaigi|ja|日本会議|12=draft}} and head of the Osaka chapter of the {{ill|Shinto Political Federation|ja|神道政治連盟|12=draft}}. The shrine is adjacent to {{ill|Katayama Park|ja|片山公園|12=draft}}.<ref>{{cite web | url=https://www.nipponkaigi.osaka/?p=831 | title=【Report】Councilors' Meeting at the Osaka Local Assembly | publisher=Nippon Kaigi Osaka | date=2012-04-01 | accessdate=2024-02-27 }}</ref>
== Grounds == * Main Hall - Rebuilt in 1984 (Shōwa 59). * Worship Hall - Rebuilt in 1984 (Shōwa 59). * Shrine Office
=== Auxiliary and Subordinate Shrines === * {{ill|Toyokawa Inari|ja|豊川稲荷|12=draft}} Shrine * Jūnishi Inari Daimyōjin - Small shrines dedicated to each of the {{ill|Chinese Zodiac|ja|十二支|12=draft}} animals. * {{ill|Atago Shrine|ja|愛宕社|12=draft}} * {{ill|Kamimusubi|ja|神産巣日神|1=Kamimusubi|lt=Kamimusubi-no-Kami|12=draft}} Shrine
== Festivals == * Autumn Festival: October 19 (Eve) and October 20 (Main Day)
== Access == * A 7-minute walk from {{ill|Suita Station (JR West)|ja|吹田駅 (JR西日本)|12=draft}} on the {{ill|JR Kyoto Line|ja|JR京都線|12=draft}} of the {{ill|West Japan Railway Company|ja|西日本旅客鉄道|12=draft}}. * A 9-minute walk from {{ill|Suita Station (Hankyu)|ja|吹田駅 (阪急)|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Hankyu Senri Line|ja|阪急千里線|12=draft}}.
== References == {{reflist}}
== Further Reading == * Shrine History of Katayama Shrine

== Access == * A 7-minute walk from {{ill|Suita Station (JR West)|ja|吹田駅 (JR西日本)|12=draft}} on the {{ill|JR Kyoto Line|ja|JR京都線|12=draft}} of the {{ill|West Japan Railway Company|ja|西日本旅客鉄道|12=draft}}. * A 9-minute walk from {{ill|Suita Station (Hankyu)|ja|吹田駅 (阪急)|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Hankyu Senri Line|ja|阪急千里線|12=draft}}.

7 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:片山神社 (吹田市) up to rev 100917136
revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 片山神社 |所在地 = [[大阪府]][[吹田市]]出口町3-3 | 緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 45 | 緯度秒 = 54 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N | 経度度 = 135 |経度分 = 31 | 経度秒 = 6.5 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E |地図国コード = JP |祭神 = [[スサノオ|素盞烏尊]]、[[天照大神|天照皇大御神]]、[[住吉三神|住吉大神]] |創建 = 不詳 |社格 = |札所等 = |本殿 = |例祭 = 秋祭り |神事 = |画像 = [[File:Katayama jinja.jpg|300px]] |地図 = }} '''片山神社'''（かたやまじんじゃ）は、[[大阪府]][[吹田市]]にある[[神社]]。正式名は「素盞嗚尊神社」。
== 祭神 == * 主祭神 - [[スサノオ|素盞烏尊]] * 相殿神 - [[天照大神|天照皇大御神]]、[[住吉三神|住吉大神]]
== 歴史 == 創建年は不詳であるが、[[平安時代]]にこの地で焼き物に携わっていた住民が、陶芸の祖神である[[スサノオ|素盞烏尊]]を祀ったのが当社の始まりと思われる。
当社は[[天正]]年間（[[1573年]] - [[1592年]]）に兵火にあって全焼し、[[1914年]]（[[大正]]3年）にも火災で焼失している。そのため、古文書等が失われて存在していないので、建立ならび由緒等については不明である。
衞藤恭宮司は[[日本会議]]大阪運営委員長と[[神道政治連盟]]大阪府本部長を務めている。また[[片山公園]]と隣接している<ref>{{cite web | url=https://www.nipponkaigi.osaka/?p=831 | title=【報告】地方議会が日本再生を発信することを誓った議員懇談会 平成24年度合同総会 | publisher=日本会議大阪 | date=2012-4-1 | accessdate=2024-2-27 }}</ref>。
== 境内 == * 本殿 - [[1984年]]（[[昭和]]59年）再建。 * 拝殿 - 1984年（昭和59年）再建。 * 社務所
=== 摂・末社 === * [[豊川稲荷]]社 * 十二支稲荷大明神 - [[十二支]]それぞれに一つづつ社が備わっている。 * [[愛宕社]] * [[カミムスビ|神産巣日神]]社
== 祭事 == * 秋祭り 10月19日（宵宮）・20日（本宮）
== 交通アクセス == * [[西日本旅客鉄道|JR]][[東海道本線]]（[[JR京都線]]） [[吹田駅 (JR西日本)|吹田駅]]より徒歩7分 * [[阪急電鉄|阪急]][[阪急千里線|千里線]] [[吹田駅 (阪急)|吹田駅]]より徒歩9分
== 脚注 == {{reflist}}
== 参考文献 == * 片山神社由緒
{{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}}
{{DEFAULTSORT:かたやましんしや}} [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:吹田市の建築物]] [[Category:吹田市の歴史]]

revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Katayama Shrine |location = [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]] [[:en:Suita|Suita City]] Deguchi-cho 3-3 | LatitudeDegrees = 34 | LatitudeMinutes = 45 | LatitudeSeconds = 54 | N/S = N | LongitudeDegrees = 135 | LongitudeMinutes = 31 | LongitudeSeconds = 6.5 | E/W = E |MapCountryCode = JP |Deities = {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞烏尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]], [[:en:Sumiyoshi sanjin|Sumiyoshi sanjin]] |Foundation = Unknown |ShrineRank = |MainHall = |AnnualFestival = Autumn Festival |Rites = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Katayama jinja.jpg|300px]] |Map = }} '''Katayama Shrine''' (片山神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Suita|Suita City]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]]. The formal name of the shrine is "Susanoo Shrine" (素盞嗚尊神社).
== Deities == * Main deity: {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞烏尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=}} * Associated deities: [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]], [[:en:Sumiyoshi sanjin|Sumiyoshi sanjin]]
== History == The year of the shrine's foundation is unknown. It is believed to have originated in the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], when local residents engaged in pottery enshrined {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞烏尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, the ancestral deity of ceramics.
The shrine was completely destroyed by fire during the [[:en:Tenshō (Momoyama period)|Tenshō Era]] (1573–1592) and again in 1914 (Taishō 3). As a result, old documents detailing its establishment and history were lost, leaving its origins unclear.
The shrine’s chief priest, Yasumi Etō, serves as chairman of the Osaka branch of the [[:en:Nippon Kaigi|Nippon Kaigi]] and head of the Osaka chapter of the {{ill|Shinto Association of Spiritual Leadership|lt=Shinto Association of Spiritual Leadership|lt=Shinto Political Federation|ja|神道政治連盟|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto Political Federation|qq=}}. The shrine is adjacent to {{ill|Katayama Park|ja|片山公園|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katayama Park|qq=}}.<ref>{{cite web | url=https://www.nipponkaigi.osaka/?p=831 | title=【Report】Councilors' Meeting at the Osaka Local Assembly | publisher=Nippon Kaigi Osaka | date=2012-04-01 | accessdate=2024-02-27 }}</ref>
== Grounds == * Main Hall - Rebuilt in 1984 (Shōwa 59). * Worship Hall - Rebuilt in 1984 (Shōwa 59). * Shrine Office
=== Auxiliary and Subordinate Shrines === * [[:en:Toyokawa Inari|Toyokawa Inari]] Shrine * Jūnishi Inari Daimyōjin - Small shrines dedicated to each of the [[:en:Earthly Branches|Chinese Zodiac]] animals. * {{ill|Atago Shrine|ja|愛宕社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atago Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Kamimusubi|ja|神産巣日神|1=Kamimusubi|lt=Kamimusubi-no-Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamimusubi|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamimusubi|qq=}} Shrine
== Festivals == * Autumn Festival: October 19 (Eve) and October 20 (Main Day)
== Access == * A 7-minute walk from [[:en:Suita Station (JR West)|Suita Station (JR West)]] on the [[:en:JR Kyōto Line|JR Kyoto Line]] of the [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|West Japan Railway Company]]. * A 9-minute walk from [[:en:Suita Station (Hankyu)|Suita Station (Hankyu)]] on the [[:en:Hankyu Senri Line|Hankyu Senri Line]].
== References == {{reflist}}
== Further Reading == * Shrine History of Katayama Shrine

revision.comment: Bot: add category Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka Prefecture
revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Katayama Shrine |location = [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]] [[:en:Suita|Suita City]] Deguchi-cho 3-3 | LatitudeDegrees = 34 | LatitudeMinutes = 45 | LatitudeSeconds = 54 | N/S = N | LongitudeDegrees = 135 | LongitudeMinutes = 31 | LongitudeSeconds = 6.5 | E/W = E |MapCountryCode = JP |Deities = {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞烏尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]], [[:en:Sumiyoshi sanjin|Sumiyoshi sanjin]] |Foundation = Unknown |ShrineRank = |MainHall = |AnnualFestival = Autumn Festival |Rites = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Katayama jinja.jpg|300px]] |Map = }} '''Katayama Shrine''' (片山神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Suita|Suita City]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]]. The formal name of the shrine is "Susanoo Shrine" (素盞嗚尊神社).
== Deities == * Main deity: {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞烏尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=}} * Associated deities: [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]], [[:en:Sumiyoshi sanjin|Sumiyoshi sanjin]]
== History == The year of the shrine's foundation is unknown. It is believed to have originated in the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], when local residents engaged in pottery enshrined {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞烏尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, the ancestral deity of ceramics.
The shrine was completely destroyed by fire during the [[:en:Tenshō (Momoyama period)|Tenshō Era]] (1573–1592) and again in 1914 (Taishō 3). As a result, old documents detailing its establishment and history were lost, leaving its origins unclear.
The shrine’s chief priest, Yasumi Etō, serves as chairman of the Osaka branch of the [[:en:Nippon Kaigi|Nippon Kaigi]] and head of the Osaka chapter of the {{ill|Shinto Association of Spiritual Leadership|lt=Shinto Association of Spiritual Leadership|lt=Shinto Political Federation|ja|神道政治連盟|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto Political Federation|qq=}}. The shrine is adjacent to {{ill|Katayama Park|ja|片山公園|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katayama Park|qq=}}.<ref>{{cite web | url=https://www.nipponkaigi.osaka/?p=831 | title=【Report】Councilors' Meeting at the Osaka Local Assembly | publisher=Nippon Kaigi Osaka | date=2012-04-01 | accessdate=2024-02-27 }}</ref>
== Grounds == * Main Hall - Rebuilt in 1984 (Shōwa 59). * Worship Hall - Rebuilt in 1984 (Shōwa 59). * Shrine Office
=== Auxiliary and Subordinate Shrines === * [[:en:Toyokawa Inari|Toyokawa Inari]] Shrine * Jūnishi Inari Daimyōjin - Small shrines dedicated to each of the [[:en:Earthly Branches|Chinese Zodiac]] animals. * {{ill|Atago Shrine|ja|愛宕社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atago Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Kamimusubi|ja|神産巣日神|1=Kamimusubi|lt=Kamimusubi-no-Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamimusubi|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamimusubi|qq=}} Shrine
== Festivals == * Autumn Festival: October 19 (Eve) and October 20 (Main Day)
== Access == * A 7-minute walk from [[:en:Suita Station (JR West)|Suita Station (JR West)]] on the [[:en:JR Kyōto Line|JR Kyoto Line]] of the [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|West Japan Railway Company]]. * A 9-minute walk from [[:en:Suita Station (Hankyu)|Suita Station (Hankyu)]] on the [[:en:Hankyu Senri Line|Hankyu Senri Line]].
== References == {{reflist}}
== Further Reading == * Shrine History of Katayama Shrine

The shrine was completely destroyed by fire during the [[:en:Tenshō (Momoyama period)|Tenshō Era]] (1573–1592) and again in 1914 (Taishō 3). As a result, old documents detailing its establishment and history were lost, leaving its origins unclear.
The shrine’s chief priest, Yasumi Etō, serves as chairman of the Osaka branch of the [[:en:Nippon Kaigi|Nippon Kaigi]] and head of the Osaka chapter of the {{ill|Shinto Association of Spiritual Leadership|lt=Shinto Association of Spiritual Leadership|lt=Shinto Political Federation|ja|神道政治連盟|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto Political Federation|qq=}}. The shrine is adjacent to {{ill|Katayama Park|ja|片山公園|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katayama Park|qq=}}.<ref>{{cite web | url=https://www.nipponkaigi.osaka/?p=831 | title=【Report】Councilors' Meeting at the Osaka Local Assembly | publisher=Nippon Kaigi Osaka | date=2012-04-01 | accessdate=2024-02-27 }}</ref>
== Grounds == * Main Hall - Rebuilt in 1984 (Shōwa 59). * Worship Hall - Rebuilt in 1984 (Shōwa 59). * Shrine Office

revision.comment: Bot: remove deleted category Shrines in Osaka Prefecture
revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Katayama Shrine |location = [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]] [[:en:Suita|Suita City]] Deguchi-cho 3-3 | LatitudeDegrees = 34 | LatitudeMinutes = 45 | LatitudeSeconds = 54 | N/S = N | LongitudeDegrees = 135 | LongitudeMinutes = 31 | LongitudeSeconds = 6.5 | E/W = E |MapCountryCode = JP |Deities = {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞烏尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]], [[:en:Sumiyoshi sanjin|Sumiyoshi sanjin]] |Foundation = Unknown |ShrineRank = |MainHall = |AnnualFestival = Autumn Festival |Rites = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Katayama jinja.jpg|300px]] |Map = }} '''Katayama Shrine''' (片山神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Suita|Suita City]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]]. The formal name of the shrine is "Susanoo Shrine" (素盞嗚尊神社).
== Deities == * Main deity: {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞烏尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * Associated deities: [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]], [[:en:Sumiyoshi sanjin|Sumiyoshi sanjin]]
== History == The year of the shrine's foundation is unknown. It is believed to have originated in the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], when local residents engaged in pottery enshrined {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞烏尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the ancestral deity of ceramics.
The shrine was completely destroyed by fire during the [[:en:Tenshō (Momoyama period)|Tenshō Era]] (1573–1592) and again in 1914 (Taishō 3). As a result, old documents detailing its establishment and history were lost, leaving its origins unclear.
The shrine’s chief priest, Yasumi Etō, serves as chairman of the Osaka branch of the [[:en:Nippon Kaigi|Nippon Kaigi]] and head of the Osaka chapter of the {{ill|Shinto Association of Spiritual Leadership|lt=Shinto Association of Spiritual Leadership|lt=Shinto Political Federation|ja|神道政治連盟|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto Political Federation|qq=}}. The shrine is adjacent to {{ill|Katayama Park|ja|片山公園|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katayama Park|qq=}}.<ref>{{cite web | url=https://www.nipponkaigi.osaka/?p=831 | title=【Report】Councilors' Meeting at the Osaka Local Assembly | publisher=Nippon Kaigi Osaka | date=2012-04-01 | accessdate=2024-02-27 }}</ref>
== Grounds == * Main Hall - Rebuilt in 1984 (Shōwa 59). * Worship Hall - Rebuilt in 1984 (Shōwa 59). * Shrine Office
=== Auxiliary and Subordinate Shrines === * [[:en:Toyokawa Inari|Toyokawa Inari]] Shrine * Jūnishi Inari Daimyōjin - Small shrines dedicated to each of the [[:en:Earthly Branches|Chinese Zodiac]] animals. * {{ill|Atago Shrine|ja|愛宕社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atago Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 愛宕権現}} * {{ill|Kamimusubi|ja|神産巣日神|1=Kamimusubi|lt=Kamimusubi-no-Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamimusubi|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamimusubi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to カミムスビ}} Shrine
== Festivals == * Autumn Festival: October 19 (Eve) and October 20 (Main Day)
== Access == * A 7-minute walk from [[:en:Suita Station (JR West)|Suita Station (JR West)]] on the [[:en:JR Kyōto Line|JR Kyoto Line]] of the [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|West Japan Railway Company]]. * A 9-minute walk from [[:en:Suita Station (Hankyu)|Suita Station (Hankyu)]] on the [[:en:Hankyu Senri Line|Hankyu Senri Line]].
== References == {{reflist}}
== Further Reading == * Shrine History of Katayama Shrine

revision.text:
{{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:26, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:05, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Katayama Shrine |location = [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]] [[:en:Suita|Suita City]] Deguchi-cho 3-3 | LatitudeDegrees = 34 | LatitudeMinutes = 45 | LatitudeSeconds = 54 | N/S = N | LongitudeDegrees = 135 | LongitudeMinutes = 31 | LongitudeSeconds = 6.5 | E/W = E |MapCountryCode = JP |Deities = {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞烏尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]], [[:en:Sumiyoshi sanjin|Sumiyoshi sanjin]] |Foundation = Unknown |ShrineRank = |MainHall = |AnnualFestival = Autumn Festival |Rites = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Katayama jinja.jpg|300px]] |Map = }} '''Katayama Shrine''' (片山神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Suita|Suita City]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]]. The formal name of the shrine is "Susanoo Shrine" (素盞嗚尊神社).
== Deities == * Main deity: {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞烏尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * Associated deities: [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]], [[:en:Sumiyoshi sanjin|Sumiyoshi sanjin]]
== History == The year of the shrine's foundation is unknown. It is believed to have originated in the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], when local residents engaged in pottery enshrined {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞烏尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the ancestral deity of ceramics.
The shrine was completely destroyed by fire during the [[:en:Tenshō (Momoyama period)|Tenshō Era]] (1573–1592) and again in 1914 (Taishō 3). As a result, old documents detailing its establishment and history were lost, leaving its origins unclear.
The shrine’s chief priest, Yasumi Etō, serves as chairman of the Osaka branch of the [[:en:Nippon Kaigi|Nippon Kaigi]] and head of the Osaka chapter of the {{ill|Shinto Association of Spiritual Leadership|lt=Shinto Association of Spiritual Leadership|lt=Shinto Political Federation|ja|神道政治連盟|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto Political Federation|qq=}}. The shrine is adjacent to {{ill|Katayama Park|ja|片山公園|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katayama Park|qq=}}.<ref>{{cite web | url=https://www.nipponkaigi.osaka/?p=831 | title=【Report】Councilors' Meeting at the Osaka Local Assembly | publisher=Nippon Kaigi Osaka | date=2012-04-01 | accessdate=2024-02-27 }}</ref>
== Grounds == * Main Hall - Rebuilt in 1984 (Shōwa 59). * Worship Hall - Rebuilt in 1984 (Shōwa 59). * Shrine Office
=== Auxiliary and Subordinate Shrines === * [[:en:Toyokawa Inari|Toyokawa Inari]] Shrine * Jūnishi Inari Daimyōjin - Small shrines dedicated to each of the [[:en:Earthly Branches|Chinese Zodiac]] animals. * {{ill|Atago Shrine|ja|愛宕社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atago Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 愛宕権現}} * {{ill|Kamimusubi|ja|神産巣日神|1=Kamimusubi|lt=Kamimusubi-no-Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamimusubi|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamimusubi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to カミムスビ}} Shrine
== Festivals == * Autumn Festival: October 19 (Eve) and October 20 (Main Day)
== Access == * A 7-minute walk from [[:en:Suita Station (JR West)|Suita Station (JR West)]] on the [[:en:JR Kyōto Line|JR Kyoto Line]] of the [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|West Japan Railway Company]]. * A 9-minute walk from [[:en:Suita Station (Hankyu)|Suita Station (Hankyu)]] on the [[:en:Hankyu Senri Line|Hankyu Senri Line]].
== References == {{reflist}}
== Further Reading == * Shrine History of Katayama Shrine

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:片山神社 (吹田市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q55522721]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:17, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:26, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:05, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Katayama Shrine |location = [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]] [[:en:Suita|Suita City]] Deguchi-cho 3-3 | LatitudeDegrees = 34 | LatitudeMinutes = 45 | LatitudeSeconds = 54 | N/S = N | LongitudeDegrees = 135 | LongitudeMinutes = 31 | LongitudeSeconds = 6.5 | E/W = E |MapCountryCode = JP |Deities = {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞烏尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]], [[:en:Sumiyoshi sanjin|Sumiyoshi sanjin]] |Foundation = Unknown |ShrineRank = |MainHall = |AnnualFestival = Autumn Festival |Rites = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Katayama jinja.jpg|300px]] |Map = }} '''Katayama Shrine''' (片山神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Suita|Suita City]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]]. The formal name of the shrine is "Susanoo Shrine" (素盞嗚尊神社).
== Deities == * Main deity: {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞烏尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * Associated deities: [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]], [[:en:Sumiyoshi sanjin|Sumiyoshi sanjin]]
== History == The year of the shrine's foundation is unknown. It is believed to have originated in the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], when local residents engaged in pottery enshrined {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞烏尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the ancestral deity of ceramics.
The shrine was completely destroyed by fire during the [[:en:Tenshō (Momoyama period)|Tenshō Era]] (1573–1592) and again in 1914 (Taishō 3). As a result, old documents detailing its establishment and history were lost, leaving its origins unclear.
The shrine’s chief priest, Yasumi Etō, serves as chairman of the Osaka branch of the [[:en:Nippon Kaigi|Nippon Kaigi]] and head of the Osaka chapter of the {{ill|Shinto Association of Spiritual Leadership|lt=Shinto Association of Spiritual Leadership|lt=Shinto Political Federation|ja|神道政治連盟|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto Political Federation|qq=}}. The shrine is adjacent to {{ill|Katayama Park|ja|片山公園|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katayama Park|qq=}}.<ref>{{cite web | url=https://www.nipponkaigi.osaka/?p=831 | title=【Report】Councilors' Meeting at the Osaka Local Assembly | publisher=Nippon Kaigi Osaka | date=2012-04-01 | accessdate=2024-02-27 }}</ref>
== Grounds == * Main Hall - Rebuilt in 1984 (Shōwa 59). * Worship Hall - Rebuilt in 1984 (Shōwa 59). * Shrine Office
=== Auxiliary and Subordinate Shrines === * [[:en:Toyokawa Inari|Toyokawa Inari]] Shrine * Jūnishi Inari Daimyōjin - Small shrines dedicated to each of the [[:en:Earthly Branches|Chinese Zodiac]] animals. * {{ill|Atago Shrine|ja|愛宕社|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atago Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 愛宕権現}} * {{ill|Kamimusubi|ja|神産巣日神|1=Kamimusubi|lt=Kamimusubi-no-Kami|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamimusubi|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamimusubi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to カミムスビ}} Shrine
== Festivals == * Autumn Festival: October 19 (Eve) and October 20 (Main Day)
== Access == * A 7-minute walk from [[:en:Suita Station (JR West)|Suita Station (JR West)]] on the [[:en:JR Kyōto Line|JR Kyoto Line]] of the [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|West Japan Railway Company]]. * A 9-minute walk from [[:en:Suita Station (Hankyu)|Suita Station (Hankyu)]] on the [[:en:Hankyu Senri Line|Hankyu Senri Line]].
== References == {{reflist}}
== Further Reading == * Shrine History of Katayama Shrine

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:片山神社 (吹田市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q55522721]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:17, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:26, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:05, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Katayama Shrine |location = [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]] [[:en:Suita|Suita City]] Deguchi-cho 3-3 | LatitudeDegrees = 34 | LatitudeMinutes = 45 | LatitudeSeconds = 54 | N/S = N | LongitudeDegrees = 135 | LongitudeMinutes = 31 | LongitudeSeconds = 6.5 | E/W = E |MapCountryCode = JP |Deities = {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞烏尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]], [[:en:Sumiyoshi sanjin|Sumiyoshi sanjin]] |Foundation = Unknown |ShrineRank = |MainHall = |AnnualFestival = Autumn Festival |Rites = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Katayama jinja.jpg|300px]] |Map = }} '''Katayama Shrine''' (片山神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Suita|Suita City]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]]. The formal name of the shrine is "Susanoo Shrine" (素盞嗚尊神社).
== Deities == * Main deity: {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞烏尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * Associated deities: [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]], [[:en:Sumiyoshi sanjin|Sumiyoshi sanjin]]
== History == The year of the shrine's foundation is unknown. It is believed to have originated in the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], when local residents engaged in pottery enshrined {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞烏尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the ancestral deity of ceramics.
The shrine was completely destroyed by fire during the [[:en:Tenshō (Momoyama period)|Tenshō Era]] (1573–1592) and again in 1914 (Taishō 3). As a result, old documents detailing its establishment and history were lost, leaving its origins unclear.
The shrine’s chief priest, Yasumi Etō, serves as chairman of the Osaka branch of the [[:en:Nippon Kaigi|Nippon Kaigi]] and head of the Osaka chapter of the {{ill|Shinto Association of Spiritual Leadership|lt=Shinto Association of Spiritual Leadership|lt=Shinto Political Federation|ja|神道政治連盟|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto Political Federation|qq=}}. The shrine is adjacent to {{ill|Katayama Park|ja|片山公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katayama Park|qq=}}.<ref>{{cite web | url=https://www.nipponkaigi.osaka/?p=831 | title=【Report】Councilors' Meeting at the Osaka Local Assembly | publisher=Nippon Kaigi Osaka | date=2012-04-01 | accessdate=2024-02-27 }}</ref>
== Grounds == * Main Hall - Rebuilt in 1984 (Shōwa 59). * Worship Hall - Rebuilt in 1984 (Shōwa 59). * Shrine Office
=== Auxiliary and Subordinate Shrines === * [[:en:Toyokawa Inari|Toyokawa Inari]] Shrine * Jūnishi Inari Daimyōjin - Small shrines dedicated to each of the [[:en:Earthly Branches|Chinese Zodiac]] animals. * {{ill|Atago Shrine|ja|愛宕社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atago Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 愛宕権現}} * {{ill|Kamimusubi|ja|神産巣日神|1=Kamimusubi|lt=Kamimusubi-no-Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamimusubi|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamimusubi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to カミムスビ}} Shrine
== Festivals == * Autumn Festival: October 19 (Eve) and October 20 (Main Day)
== Access == * A 7-minute walk from [[:en:Suita Station (JR West)|Suita Station (JR West)]] on the [[:en:JR Kyōto Line|JR Kyoto Line]] of the [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|West Japan Railway Company]]. * A 9-minute walk from [[:en:Suita Station (Hankyu)|Suita Station (Hankyu)]] on the [[:en:Hankyu Senri Line|Hankyu Senri Line]].
== References == {{reflist}}
== Further Reading == * Shrine History of Katayama Shrine

The shrine was completely destroyed by fire during the [[:en:Tenshō (Momoyama period)|Tenshō Era]] (1573–1592) and again in 1914 (Taishō 3). As a result, old documents detailing its establishment and history were lost, leaving its origins unclear.
The shrine’s chief priest, Yasumi Etō, serves as chairman of the Osaka branch of the [[:en:Nippon Kaigi|Nippon Kaigi]] and head of the Osaka chapter of the {{ill|Shinto Association of Spiritual Leadership|lt=Shinto Association of Spiritual Leadership|lt=Shinto Political Federation|ja|神道政治連盟|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto Political Federation|qq=}}. The shrine is adjacent to {{ill|Katayama Park|ja|片山公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katayama Park|qq=}}.<ref>{{cite web | url=https://www.nipponkaigi.osaka/?p=831 | title=【Report】Councilors' Meeting at the Osaka Local Assembly | publisher=Nippon Kaigi Osaka | date=2012-04-01 | accessdate=2024-02-27 }}</ref>
== Grounds == * Main Hall - Rebuilt in 1984 (Shōwa 59). * Worship Hall - Rebuilt in 1984 (Shōwa 59). * Shrine Office
=== Auxiliary and Subordinate Shrines === * [[:en:Toyokawa Inari|Toyokawa Inari]] Shrine * Jūnishi Inari Daimyōjin - Small shrines dedicated to each of the [[:en:Earthly Branches|Chinese Zodiac]] animals. * {{ill|Atago Shrine|ja|愛宕社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atago Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 愛宕権現}} * {{ill|Kamimusubi|ja|神産巣日神|1=Kamimusubi|lt=Kamimusubi-no-Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamimusubi|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamimusubi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to カミムスビ}} Shrine
== Festivals == * Autumn Festival: October 19 (Eve) and October 20 (Main Day)
== Access == * A 7-minute walk from [[:en:Suita Station (JR West)|Suita Station (JR West)]] on the [[:en:JR Kyōto Line|JR Kyoto Line]] of the [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|West Japan Railway Company]]. * A 9-minute walk from [[:en:Suita Station (Hankyu)|Suita Station (Hankyu)]] on the [[:en:Hankyu Senri Line|Hankyu Senri Line]].
== References == {{reflist}}
== Further Reading == * Shrine History of Katayama Shrine

== Access == * A 7-minute walk from [[:en:Suita Station (JR West)|Suita Station (JR West)]] on the [[:en:JR Kyōto Line|JR Kyoto Line]] of the [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|West Japan Railway Company]]. * A 9-minute walk from [[:en:Suita Station (Hankyu)|Suita Station (Hankyu)]] on the [[:en:Hankyu Senri Line|Hankyu Senri Line]].

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:片山神社 (吹田市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:片山神社 (吹田市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:片山神社 (吹田市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:片山神社 (吹田市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q55522721]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:17, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:26, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:05, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Katayama Shrine |location = [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]] [[:en:Suita|Suita City]] Deguchi-cho 3-3 | LatitudeDegrees = 34 | LatitudeMinutes = 45 | LatitudeSeconds = 54 | N/S = N | LongitudeDegrees = 135 | LongitudeMinutes = 31 | LongitudeSeconds = 6.5 | E/W = E |MapCountryCode = JP |Deities = {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞烏尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]], [[:en:Sumiyoshi sanjin|Sumiyoshi sanjin]] |Foundation = Unknown |ShrineRank = |MainHall = |AnnualFestival = Autumn Festival |Rites = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Katayama jinja.jpg|300px]] |Map = }} '''Katayama Shrine''' (片山神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Suita|Suita City]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]]. The formal name of the shrine is "Susanoo Shrine" (素盞嗚尊神社).
== Deities == * Main deity: {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞烏尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * Associated deities: [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]], [[:en:Sumiyoshi sanjin|Sumiyoshi sanjin]]
== History == The year of the shrine's foundation is unknown. It is believed to have originated in the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], when local residents engaged in pottery enshrined {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞烏尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the ancestral deity of ceramics.
The shrine was completely destroyed by fire during the [[:en:Tenshō (Momoyama period)|Tenshō Era]] (1573–1592) and again in 1914 (Taishō 3). As a result, old documents detailing its establishment and history were lost, leaving its origins unclear.
The shrine’s chief priest, Yasumi Etō, serves as chairman of the Osaka branch of the [[:en:Nippon Kaigi|Nippon Kaigi]] and head of the Osaka chapter of the {{ill|Shinto Association of Spiritual Leadership|lt=Shinto Association of Spiritual Leadership|lt=Shinto Political Federation|ja|神道政治連盟|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto Political Federation|qq=}}. The shrine is adjacent to {{ill|Katayama Park|ja|片山公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katayama Park|qq=}}.<ref>{{cite web | url=https://www.nipponkaigi.osaka/?p=831 | title=【Report】Councilors' Meeting at the Osaka Local Assembly | publisher=Nippon Kaigi Osaka | date=2012-04-01 | accessdate=2024-02-27 }}</ref>
== Grounds == * Main Hall - Rebuilt in 1984 (Shōwa 59). * Worship Hall - Rebuilt in 1984 (Shōwa 59). * Shrine Office
=== Auxiliary and Subordinate Shrines === * [[:en:Toyokawa Inari|Toyokawa Inari]] Shrine * Jūnishi Inari Daimyōjin - Small shrines dedicated to each of the [[:en:Earthly Branches|Chinese Zodiac]] animals. * {{ill|Atago Shrine|ja|愛宕社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atago Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 愛宕権現}} * {{ill|Kamimusubi|ja|神産巣日神|1=Kamimusubi|lt=Kamimusubi-no-Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamimusubi|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamimusubi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to カミムスビ}} Shrine
== Festivals == * Autumn Festival: October 19 (Eve) and October 20 (Main Day)
== Access == * A 7-minute walk from [[:en:Suita Station (JR West)|Suita Station (JR West)]] on the [[:en:JR Kyōto Line|JR Kyoto Line]] of the [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|West Japan Railway Company]]. * A 9-minute walk from [[:en:Suita Station (Hankyu)|Suita Station (Hankyu)]] on the [[:en:Hankyu Senri Line|Hankyu Senri Line]].

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:片山神社 (吹田市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:片山神社 (吹田市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:片山神社 (吹田市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:片山神社 (吹田市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q55522721]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:17, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:26, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:05, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Katayama Shrine |location = [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]] [[:en:Suita|Suita City]] Deguchi-cho 3-3 | LatitudeDegrees = 34 | LatitudeMinutes = 45 | LatitudeSeconds = 54 | N/S = N | LongitudeDegrees = 135 | LongitudeMinutes = 31 | LongitudeSeconds = 6.5 | E/W = E |MapCountryCode = JP |Deities = {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞烏尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]], [[:en:Sumiyoshi sanjin|Sumiyoshi sanjin]] |Foundation = Unknown |ShrineRank = |MainHall = |AnnualFestival = Autumn Festival |Rites = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Katayama jinja.jpg|300px]] |Map = }} '''Katayama Shrine''' (片山神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Suita|Suita City]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]]. The formal name of the shrine is "Susanoo Shrine" (素盞嗚尊神社).
== Deities == * Main deity: {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞烏尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * Associated deities: [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]], [[:en:Sumiyoshi sanjin|Sumiyoshi sanjin]]
== History == The year of the shrine's foundation is unknown. It is believed to have originated in the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], when local residents engaged in pottery enshrined {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞烏尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the ancestral deity of ceramics.
The shrine was completely destroyed by fire during the [[:en:Tenshō (Momoyama period)|Tenshō Era]] (1573–1592) and again in 1914 (Taishō 3). As a result, old documents detailing its establishment and history were lost, leaving its origins unclear.
The shrine’s chief priest, Yasumi Etō, serves as chairman of the Osaka branch of the [[:en:Nippon Kaigi|Nippon Kaigi]] and head of the Osaka chapter of the {{ill|Shinto Association of Spiritual Leadership|lt=Shinto Association of Spiritual Leadership|lt=Shinto Political Federation|ja|神道政治連盟|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto Political Federation|qq=}}. The shrine is adjacent to {{ill|Katayama Park|ja|片山公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katayama Park|qq=}}.<ref>{{cite web | url=https://www.nipponkaigi.osaka/?p=831 | title=【Report】Councilors' Meeting at the Osaka Local Assembly | publisher=Nippon Kaigi Osaka | date=2012-04-01 | accessdate=2024-02-27 }}</ref>
== Grounds == * Main Hall - Rebuilt in 1984 (Shōwa 59). * Worship Hall - Rebuilt in 1984 (Shōwa 59). * Shrine Office
=== Auxiliary and Subordinate Shrines === * [[:en:Toyokawa Inari|Toyokawa Inari]] Shrine * Jūnishi Inari Daimyōjin - Small shrines dedicated to each of the [[:en:Earthly Branches|Chinese Zodiac]] animals. * {{ill|Atago Shrine|ja|愛宕社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atago Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 愛宕権現}} * {{ill|Kamimusubi|ja|神産巣日神|1=Kamimusubi|lt=Kamimusubi-no-Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamimusubi|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamimusubi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to カミムスビ}} Shrine
== Festivals == * Autumn Festival: October 19 (Eve) and October 20 (Main Day)
== Access == * A 7-minute walk from [[:en:Suita Station (JR West)|Suita Station (JR West)]] on the [[:en:JR Kyōto Line|JR Kyoto Line]] of the [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|West Japan Railway Company]]. * A 9-minute walk from [[:en:Suita Station (Hankyu)|Suita Station (Hankyu)]] on the [[:en:Hankyu Senri Line|Hankyu Senri Line]].
== References == {{reflist}}
== Further Reading == * Shrine History of Katayama Shrine

== Access == * A 7-minute walk from {{ill|Suita Station (JR West)|en|Suita Station (JR West)|WD=Q59351|lt=Suita Station (JR West)}} on the {{ill|JR Kyōto Line|en|JR Kyōto Line|WD=Q1195820|lt=JR Kyoto Line}} of the {{ill|West Japan Railway Company|en|West Japan Railway Company|WD=Q502125|lt=West Japan Railway Company}}. * A 9-minute walk from {{ill|Suita Station (Hankyu)|en|Suita Station (Hankyu)|WD=Q7635540|lt=Suita Station (Hankyu)}} on the {{ill|Hankyu Senri Line|en|Hankyu Senri Line|WD=Q1044987|lt=Hankyu Senri Line}}.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:片山神社 (吹田市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:片山神社 (吹田市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:片山神社 (吹田市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:片山神社 (吹田市)]]
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Katayama Shrine |location = {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|WD=Q122723|lt=Osaka Prefecture}} {{ill|Suita|en|Suita|WD=Q653510|lt=Suita City}} Deguchi-cho 3-3 | LatitudeDegrees = 34 | LatitudeMinutes = 45 | LatitudeSeconds = 54 | N/S = N | LongitudeDegrees = 135 | LongitudeMinutes = 31 | LongitudeSeconds = 6.5 | E/W = E |MapCountryCode = JP |Deities = {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞烏尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Amaterasu|en|Amaterasu|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami}}, {{ill|Sumiyoshi sanjin|en|Sumiyoshi sanjin|WD=Q1114013|lt=Sumiyoshi sanjin}} |Foundation = Unknown |ShrineRank = |MainHall = |AnnualFestival = Autumn Festival |Rites = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Katayama jinja.jpg|300px]] |Map = }} '''Katayama Shrine''' (片山神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Suita|en|Suita|WD=Q653510|lt=Suita City}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|WD=Q122723|lt=Osaka Prefecture}}. The formal name of the shrine is "Susanoo Shrine" (素盞嗚尊神社).
== Deities == * Main deity: {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞烏尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * Associated deities: {{ill|Amaterasu|en|Amaterasu|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami}}, {{ill|Sumiyoshi sanjin|en|Sumiyoshi sanjin|WD=Q1114013|lt=Sumiyoshi sanjin}}
== History == The year of the shrine's foundation is unknown. It is believed to have originated in the {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period}}, when local residents engaged in pottery enshrined {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞烏尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the ancestral deity of ceramics.
The shrine was completely destroyed by fire during the {{ill|Tenshō (Momoyama period)|en|Tenshō (Momoyama period)|WD=Q1145058|lt=Tenshō Era}} (1573–1592) and again in 1914 (Taishō 3). As a result, old documents detailing its establishment and history were lost, leaving its origins unclear.
The shrine’s chief priest, Yasumi Etō, serves as chairman of the Osaka branch of the {{ill|Nippon Kaigi|en|Nippon Kaigi|WD=Q11507216|lt=Nippon Kaigi}} and head of the Osaka chapter of the {{ill|Shinto Association of Spiritual Leadership|lt=Shinto Association of Spiritual Leadership|lt=Shinto Political Federation|ja|神道政治連盟|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto Political Federation|qq=}}. The shrine is adjacent to {{ill|Katayama Park|ja|片山公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katayama Park|qq=}}.<ref>{{cite web | url=https://www.nipponkaigi.osaka/?p=831 | title=【Report】Councilors' Meeting at the Osaka Local Assembly | publisher=Nippon Kaigi Osaka | date=2012-04-01 | accessdate=2024-02-27 }}</ref>
== Grounds == * Main Hall - Rebuilt in 1984 (Shōwa 59). * Worship Hall - Rebuilt in 1984 (Shōwa 59). * Shrine Office
=== Auxiliary and Subordinate Shrines === * {{ill|Toyokawa Inari|en|Toyokawa Inari|WD=Q3080596|lt=Toyokawa Inari}} Shrine * Jūnishi Inari Daimyōjin - Small shrines dedicated to each of the {{ill|Earthly Branches|en|Earthly Branches|WD=Q1072961|lt=Chinese Zodiac}} animals. * {{ill|Atago Shrine|ja|愛宕社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atago Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 愛宕権現}} * {{ill|Kamimusubi|ja|神産巣日神|1=Kamimusubi|lt=Kamimusubi-no-Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamimusubi|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamimusubi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to カミムスビ}} Shrine
== Festivals == * Autumn Festival: October 19 (Eve) and October 20 (Main Day)
== Access == * A 7-minute walk from {{ill|Suita Station (JR West)|en|Suita Station (JR West)|WD=Q59351|lt=Suita Station (JR West)}} on the {{ill|JR Kyōto Line|en|JR Kyōto Line|WD=Q1195820|lt=JR Kyoto Line}} of the {{ill|West Japan Railway Company|en|West Japan Railway Company|WD=Q502125|lt=West Japan Railway Company}}. * A 9-minute walk from {{ill|Suita Station (Hankyu)|en|Suita Station (Hankyu)|WD=Q7635540|lt=Suita Station (Hankyu)}} on the {{ill|Hankyu Senri Line|en|Hankyu Senri Line|WD=Q1044987|lt=Hankyu Senri Line}}.

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Katayama Shrine |location = {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|WD=Q122723|lt=Osaka Prefecture|1=Osaka Prefecture}} {{ill|Suita|en|Suita|WD=Q653510|lt=Suita City|1=Suita}} Deguchi-cho 3-3 | LatitudeDegrees = 34 | LatitudeMinutes = 45 | LatitudeSeconds = 54 | N/S = N | LongitudeDegrees = 135 | LongitudeMinutes = 31 | LongitudeSeconds = 6.5 | E/W = E |MapCountryCode = JP |Deities = {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞烏尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Amaterasu|en|Amaterasu|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|1=Amaterasu}}, {{ill|Sumiyoshi sanjin|en|Sumiyoshi sanjin|WD=Q1114013|lt=Sumiyoshi sanjin|1=Sumiyoshi sanjin}} |Foundation = Unknown |ShrineRank = |MainHall = |AnnualFestival = Autumn Festival |Rites = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Katayama jinja.jpg|300px]] |Map = }} '''Katayama Shrine''' (片山神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Suita|en|Suita|WD=Q653510|lt=Suita City|1=Suita}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|WD=Q122723|lt=Osaka Prefecture|1=Osaka Prefecture}}. The formal name of the shrine is "Susanoo Shrine" (素盞嗚尊神社).
== Deities == * Main deity: {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞烏尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * Associated deities: {{ill|Amaterasu|en|Amaterasu|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|1=Amaterasu}}, {{ill|Sumiyoshi sanjin|en|Sumiyoshi sanjin|WD=Q1114013|lt=Sumiyoshi sanjin|1=Sumiyoshi sanjin}}
== History == The year of the shrine's foundation is unknown. It is believed to have originated in the {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period|1=Heian period}}, when local residents engaged in pottery enshrined {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞烏尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the ancestral deity of ceramics.
The shrine was completely destroyed by fire during the {{ill|Tenshō (Momoyama period)|en|Tenshō (Momoyama period)|WD=Q1145058|lt=Tenshō Era|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} (1573–1592) and again in 1914 (Taishō 3). As a result, old documents detailing its establishment and history were lost, leaving its origins unclear.
The shrine’s chief priest, Yasumi Etō, serves as chairman of the Osaka branch of the {{ill|Nippon Kaigi|en|Nippon Kaigi|WD=Q11507216|lt=Nippon Kaigi|1=Nippon Kaigi}} and head of the Osaka chapter of the {{ill|Shinto Association of Spiritual Leadership|lt=Shinto Association of Spiritual Leadership|lt=Shinto Political Federation|ja|神道政治連盟|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto Political Federation|qq=}}. {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Katayama Shrine |location = {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|qid=Q122723|lt=Osaka Prefecture|1=Osaka Prefecture}} {{ill|Suita|en|Suita|qid=Q653510|lt=Suita City|1=Suita}} Deguchi-cho 3-3 | LatitudeDegrees = 34 | LatitudeMinutes = 45 | LatitudeSeconds = 54 | N/S = N | LongitudeDegrees = 135 | LongitudeMinutes = 31 | LongitudeSeconds = 6.5 | E/W = E |MapCountryCode = JP |Deities = {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞烏尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Amaterasu|en|Amaterasu|qid=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|1=Amaterasu}}, {{ill|Sumiyoshi sanjin|en|Sumiyoshi sanjin|qid=Q1114013|lt=Sumiyoshi sanjin|1=Sumiyoshi sanjin}} |Foundation = Unknown |ShrineRank = |MainHall = |AnnualFestival = Autumn Festival |Rites = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Katayama jinja.jpg|300px]] |Map = }} '''Katayama Shrine''' (片山神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|qid=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Suita|en|Suita|qid=Q653510|lt=Suita City|1=Suita}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|qid=Q122723|lt=Osaka Prefecture|1=Osaka Prefecture}}. The formal name of the shrine is "Susanoo Shrine" (素盞嗚尊神社).
== Deities == * Main deity: {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞烏尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * Associated deities: {{ill|Amaterasu|en|Amaterasu|qid=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|1=Amaterasu}}, {{ill|Sumiyoshi sanjin|en|Sumiyoshi sanjin|qid=Q1114013|lt=Sumiyoshi sanjin|1=Sumiyoshi sanjin}}
== History == The year of the shrine's foundation is unknown. It is believed to have originated in the {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|qid=Q193292|lt=Heian period|1=Heian period}}, when local residents engaged in pottery enshrined {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞烏尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the ancestral deity of ceramics.
The shrine was completely destroyed by fire during the {{ill|Tenshō (Momoyama period)|en|Tenshō (Momoyama period)|qid=Q1145058|lt=Tenshō Era|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} (1573–1592) and again in 1914 (Taishō 3). As a result, old documents detailing its establishment and history were lost, leaving its origins unclear.
The shrine’s chief priest, Yasumi Etō, serves as chairman of the Osaka branch of the {{ill|Nippon Kaigi|en|Nippon Kaigi|qid=Q11507216|lt=Nippon Kaigi|1=Nippon Kaigi}} and head of the Osaka chapter of the {{ill|Shinto Association of Spiritual Leadership|lt=Shinto Association of Spiritual Leadership|lt=Shinto Political Federation|ja|神道政治連盟|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto Political Federation|qq=}}. The shrine is adjacent to {{ill|Katayama Park|ja|片山公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katayama Park|qq=}}.<ref>{{cite web | url=https://www.nipponkaigi.osaka/?p=831 | title=【Report】Councilors' Meeting at the Osaka Local Assembly | publisher=Nippon Kaigi Osaka | date=2012-04-01 | accessdate=2024-02-27 }}</ref>
== Grounds == * Main Hall - Rebuilt in 1984 (Shōwa 59). * Worship Hall - Rebuilt in 1984 (Shōwa 59). * Shrine Office
=== Auxiliary and Subordinate Shrines === * {{ill|Toyokawa Inari|en|Toyokawa Inari|qid=Q3080596|lt=Toyokawa Inari|1=Toyokawa Inari}} Shrine * Jūnishi Inari Daimyōjin - Small shrines dedicated to each of the {{ill|Earthly Branches|en|Earthly Branches|qid=Q1072961|lt=Chinese Zodiac|1=Earthly Branches}} animals. * {{ill|Atago Shrine|ja|愛宕社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atago Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 愛宕権現}} * {{ill|Kamimusubi|ja|神産巣日神|1=Kamimusubi|qid=Q3275901|lt=Kamimusubi-no-Kami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamimusubi|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamimusubi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to カミムスビ}} Shrine
== Festivals == * Autumn Festival: October 19 (Eve) and October 20 (Main Day)
== Access == * A 7-minute walk from {{ill|Suita Station (JR West)|en|Suita Station (JR West)|qid=Q59351|lt=Suita Station (JR West)|1=Suita Station (JR West)}} on the {{ill|JR Kyōto Line|en|JR Kyōto Line|qid=Q1195820|lt=JR Kyoto Line|1=JR Kyōto Line}} of the {{ill|West Japan Railway Company|en|West Japan Railway Company|qid=Q502125|lt=West Japan Railway Company|1=West Japan Railway Company}}. * A 9-minute walk from {{ill|Suita Station (Hankyu)|en|Suita Station (Hankyu)|qid=Q7635540|lt=Suita Station (Hankyu)|1=Suita Station (Hankyu)}} on the {{ill|Hankyu Senri Line|en|Hankyu Senri Line|qid=Q1044987|lt=Hankyu Senri Line|1=Hankyu Senri Line}}.
